name: INGOP
subject: Building Momentum
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
Dear Hoosier Republicans,

No images? Click here https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-e-anhkly-hhlljjcji-k/

Building Momentum

Dear Hoosier Republicans,

This week, Indiana continues to show what strong conservative leadership delivers. Governor Braun’s Main Street strategy is driving real results across the state, supporting small businesses, increasing wages, and protecting taxpayers. With more than 591,000 small businesses employing 1.2 million Hoosiers, Indiana remains a national leader in job creation and business growth. By prioritizing entrepreneurs and demanding accountability in economic development, Governor Braun is building an economy that works for working families and keeps Indiana competitive, even as other states struggle.

Meanwhile, President Trump’s America First policies are producing strong private-sector job growth, rising wages, and renewed confidence in the economy, while targeted relief for farmers is helping stabilize markets and lower costs for families.

As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's any way I can be of assistance.

Yours in service,

Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman

Governor Braun Completes 92 County Tour

Governor Braun completed his seventh annual 92 County Tour, reaffirming his commitment to accessibility and desire to hear directly from every Hoosier community.

"My annual 92 County Tour is all about hearing from Hoosiers about the kitchen table issues that matter most to them, and talking about the results my administration is getting on priorities like increasing jobs and wages and lowering energy prices. I am committed to ensuring our efforts benefit all corners of Indiana, and we're showing up everywhere to demonstrate that commitment."

- Governor Braun via a press release [https://events.in.gov/event/gov-braun-completes-seventh-annual-tour-of-all-92-counties]

Governor Braun Delivers on Economic Development Strategy

Governor Braun announced a strong year for Indiana’s economy, with companies committing to create more than 10,600 new jobs in 202, with an average wage of $40.59 an hour. At the same time, the state cut the average incentive cost per job from $46,850 to $15,485, delivering major taxpayer savings. Indiana also expanded support for entrepreneurs through the new Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation and advanced READI 2.0 investments to strengthen every Hoosier community.

"Today’s forecast is evidence that our focus on jobs and wages to grow the economy is working. We are boosting Hoosier wages and lowering taxes. While many other states are struggling to gain momentum, Indiana is thriving and Hoosiers are keeping more money in their pockets."

- Governor Braun via a press release [https://events.in.gov/event/statement-hoosier-economy-thrives-under-gov-mike-brauns-leadership-7510]

Congress of Counties 2026

Click Here for More Information [http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/ycek62v7j3igMr-xrrBJi-8L08RjGlBYAVisamv_wHiTRsR5560b59e0K6wMsMed3pkfqgXRd5EkUwjnmasIu1R8q4Gg0-BWJyX8TclkmM6VS3BsNYWXinnOhjK5rm0VYdyolx5uzBgpqW3wjm5LuKrSrTPtzY8R_ORR54NfS-s6p5932coO3Ur5364_jmgmiwkuv8MGNuK5qWmsyCKLC5p3gR9JtvFZXy9dE5e5GGDvqaitJaBxEjXDHRJ-43SOfHXmayIjMaDQdc0P-UU/M_qqHzk9XkTw8X0bVqVzFm3KJCoTJsJI/12] Click Here to Register [http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/7S_sOJvegMt5UhJcQAKGLRFp8wXiU5lbKhJMbyGxRt5PjYXL_7pw4ckv3pjTjh8YuR9RZ2MwxytY5TMDqgUe-LieOp_Gb6QvL32mtZUlQ2kTY0sSWrIXzElIsnufFQaGiLM5WYeLrFM_6-yKsWfd5LDuR7xfEjkH5CXXWwQoCzkn_664dJTa9QhU8tpEsolf7e5JQzwBFK9nxzpHU7x42d4wAaGkCNSmUnuetg9MNsm6pvqP132ED_4J2gzcoXqoPZPvZ7u0IoSqrtdwaMzibLmMmaoiPZ4/EravEEHK8vzS31iu4ISLQWl8IzXuG-TH/12]

Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Spring Semester

📱 News You Can Use

Governor Mike Braun congratulated [https://x.com/GovBraun/status/2001451304657306073] New Palestine Elementary School Principal Kayleigh Fosnow on receiving the prestigious Milken Educator Award, often referred to as the “Oscars of Teaching.” Selected from educators nationwide, her recognition reflects Indiana’s commitment to excellence in education.

Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith highlighted [https://x.com/LGMicahBeckwith/status/2001376553867923881] a $750,000 Lilly Endowment grant to upgrade the Indiana FFA Leadership Center in Trafalgar, strengthening safety and hands-on leadership training. The investment reflects Hoosiers helping Hoosiers and preparing students to lead in the real world.

Secretary Diego Morales welcomed [https://x.com/SOSDiegoMorales/status/2000959178098250222] election officials from across Indiana to the Indiana Election Administrators’ Conference and thanked them for their dedication to safeguarding elections on Election Day and beyond.

Treasurer Daniel Elliott toured [https://x.com/TOS_Elliott/status/2001017398124835076] Easterseals Crossroads with CEO David Dreith and Director Elizabeth McFarlane, highlighting the state’s partnership through INvestABLE Indiana.

Attorney General Todd Rokita attended [https://events.in.gov/event/attorney-general-todd-rokita-attends-trump-administration-announcement-ofbold-actions-to-stop-sex-rejecting-procedures-on-minors?utm_campaign=widget&utm_medium=widget&utm_source=State+of+Indiana] a Trump administration announcement on new federal actions to protect children from sex-rejecting medical procedures. He emphasized Indiana’s commitment to commonsense protections and his continued defense of state laws safeguarding minors.

State Comptroller Elise Nieshalla praised [https://x.com/Comp_Nieshalla/status/2000612739484623081] fiduciary responsibility and thanked Donald Trump for an executive order protecting pension returns. She noted Indiana’s leadership in stopping politically driven proxy voting that prioritizes ESG and DEI over retirees’ interests.

Senator Todd Young voted [https://www.young.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/young-votes-for-annual-defense-bill-to-support-our-military-keep-america-secure/] for the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act, strengthening national security and supporting service members. The bill also includes his effort to repeal outdated Iraq War authorizations and close the chapter on “forever wars.”

Senator Jim Banks voted [https://www.banks.senate.gov/news/press-releases/banks-votes-to-pass-the-fy-2026-ndaa-delivers-key-wins-for-indiana/] to pass the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act, delivering key investments that strengthen national security and support Hoosier jobs. The bill secures major funding for Indiana defense facilities and innovation, reinforcing the state’s leadership in military readiness and technology.

Congressman Rudy Yakym encouraged [https://yakym.house.gov/posts/rep-yakym-encourages-small-businesses-to-apply-for-sba-awards] small business owners in Indiana’s Second District to apply for the 2026 National Small Business Week Awards by the December 22 deadline. The program recognizes entrepreneurs who play a vital role in strengthening local communities and the economy.

Congressman Marlin Stutzman advanced [https://stutzman.house.gov/media/press-releases/house-financial-services-committee-advances-two-stutzman-bills] two bills through the House Financial Services Committee aimed at cutting red tape and boosting economic growth. The legislation supports small and mid-sized businesses while streamlining rural housing development to make affordable homes easier to build.

Congressman Jim Baird met [https://x.com/RepJimBaird/status/2001392564599919070] with members of the Indiana National Guard stationed in Washington, D.C., and thanked them for their service. He expressed appreciation for their continued dedication to Indiana and the nation.

Congresswoman Victoria Spartz spoke [https://x.com/RepSpartz/status/2001454759308362079] at a Joint Economic Committee Republicans hearing on health care costs, highlighting how government incentives and barriers have driven consolidation and rising prices.

Congressman Jefferson Shreve led [https://shreve.house.gov/media/press-releases/congressman-shreve-calls-coordinated-us-response-china-and-russias-growing] a letter urging the State Department to pursue a coordinated U.S. strategy to counter the growing influence of China and Russia in Latin America. He emphasized strengthening partnerships with reform-minded democratic nations to protect U.S. interests and democratic values.

Congressman Mark Messmer requested [https://x.com/RepMessmer/status/2001348684257333295] that the U.S. Navy name a future warship after Evansville, honoring the city’s long history of supporting American troops. He said Evansville’s legacy makes it deserving of the honor of the USS Evansville.

Congresswoman Erin Houchin announced [https://houchin.house.gov/media/press-releases/houchin-announces-service-academy-nominations-indianas-9th-district-students] service academy nominations for outstanding students from Indiana’s 9th District. The nominees were selected through a competitive process recognizing academic excellence, leadership, and a commitment to serving the nation.

Upcoming Events

February 4: Washington Township Lincoln Day Dinner [https://indiana.gop/event/washington-township-gop-lincoln-day-dinner/]

February 7: Pulaski County Lincoln Day [https://indiana.gop/event/pulaski-countys-lincoln-day/]

February 20: Congress of Counties 2026 [http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/ByGAklaSpSlaSFTWkudsdD3rezGzyBqL_A3jEDmiUY-EdRVlN9wdMWOz0PeMA2klryViYJwOnTpbFYaXUX58Yl8LfjI8iA5VKN_aGBvdqOXbCiGsYFYnZSrefJQD-G6p63ouDUvBigs3i1WLntfG8usYXEiROfMBGVWyXzsXBkDZDVGpI3sOfKGioIx-prrfbrWv_Qf14r69yofDZdAJEWVlqH_QgXqRwz9SRgLcOkWhK5GPVkQwa1tNlVcw24wNDRcJZ_418igvXJx6R9E6VZILLDsvWBjyswbiAR8e/r81NUxMkFIg5u_9zsPWDlmZGVWOVKB1u/12]

February 28: Carroll County Lincoln Day Dinner [https://indiana.gop/event/carroll-county-lincoln-day-dinner/]

April 10: Dearborn County Lincoln Day Dinner [https://indiana.gop/event/dearborn-county-lincoln-day-dinner-2/]

June 19: Indiana Republican State Convention 2026 [http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/vnfyzglVyuaQFDny4fn0_EF2ELHcyiTrX8WUKRBdDiVS-sC87d7iqYSkXPhzknowviPogMCKdy6aR9i-2sNC5AyEmpqddZSKP59GnGq4s-VoMjA_OwY6AudqvWLXGouk-pmn6kUAkI7pSLeuo6_yZyJvPV3Z3qO5Mri_XoxbrNB2V3n3z_MfBxEumlXtVsFcBrPGaXnUgVuicb7UHnHUwF7Tqz5kZ2am6GPAlII0iSQeouOaQS4ScrvxFPNer38eNPVUDfTMRYcg0btRpd-NYAyc12fSFzlJJPatjswNK2213ItJYcAs0FSbbq5I/4-j1SM9LG10fvtqF_4XCmFhZm-97I0AU/12]

Yours in service, Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman Governor Braun Completes 92 County Tour Governor Braun completed his seventh annual 92 County Tour, reaffirming his commitment to accessibility and desire to hear directly from every Hoosier community. "My annual 92 County Tour is all about hearing from Hoosiers about the kitchen table issues that matter most to them, and talking about the results my administration is getting on priorities like increasing jobs and wages and lowering energy prices. I am committed to ensuring our efforts benefit all corners of Indiana, and we're showing up everywhere to demonstrate that commitment." - Governor Braun via a press release [] Governor Braun Delivers on Economic Development Strategy Governor Braun announced a strong year for Indiana’s economy, with companies committing to create more than 10,600 new jobs in 202, with an average wage of $40.59 an hour. At the same time, the state cut the average incentive cost per job from $46,850 to $15,485, delivering major taxpayer savings. Indiana also expanded support for entrepreneurs through the new Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation and advanced READI 2.0 investments to strengthen every Hoosier community. "Today’s forecast is evidence that our focus on jobs and wages to grow the economy is working. We are boosting Hoosier wages and lowering taxes. While many other states are struggling to gain momentum, Indiana is thriving and Hoosiers are keeping more money in their pockets." - Governor Braun via a press release [] Congress of Counties 2026 Click Here for More Information [] Click Here to Register [] Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Spring Semester 📱 News You Can Use Governor Mike Braun congratulated [] New Palestine Elementary School Principal Kayleigh Fosnow on receiving the prestigious Milken Educator Award, often referred to as the “Oscars of Teaching.” Selected from educators nationwide, her recognition reflects Indiana’s commitment to excellence in education. Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith highlighted [] a $750,000 Lilly Endowment grant to upgrade the Indiana FFA Leadership Center in Trafalgar, strengthening safety and hands-on leadership training. The investment reflects Hoosiers helping Hoosiers and preparing students to lead in the real world. Secretary Diego Morales welcomed [] election officials from across Indiana to the Indiana Election Administrators’ Conference and thanked them for their dedication to safeguarding elections on Election Day and beyond. Treasurer Daniel Elliott toured [] Easterseals Crossroads with CEO David Dreith and Director Elizabeth McFarlane, highlighting the state’s partnership through INvestABLE Indiana. Attorney General Todd Rokita attended [] a Trump administration announcement on new federal actions to protect children from sex-rejecting medical procedures. He emphasized Indiana’s commitment to commonsense protections and his continued defense of state laws safeguarding minors. State Comptroller Elise Nieshalla praised [] fiduciary responsibility and thanked Donald Trump for an executive order protecting pension returns. She noted Indiana’s leadership in stopping politically driven proxy voting that prioritizes ESG and DEI over retirees’ interests. Senator Todd Young voted [] for the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act, strengthening national security and supporting service members. The bill also includes his effort to repeal outdated Iraq War authorizations and close the chapter on “forever wars.” Senator Jim Banks voted [] to pass the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act, delivering key investments that strengthen national security and support Hoosier jobs. The bill secures major funding for Indiana defense facilities and innovation, reinforcing the state’s leadership in military readiness and technology. Congressman Rudy Yakym encouraged [] small business owners in Indiana’s Second District to apply for the 2026 National Small Business Week Awards by the December 22 deadline. The program recognizes entrepreneurs who play a vital role in strengthening local communities and the economy. Congressman Marlin Stutzman advanced [] two bills through the House Financial Services Committee aimed at cutting red tape and boosting economic growth. The legislation supports small and mid-sized businesses while streamlining rural housing development to make affordable homes easier to build. Congressman Jim Baird met [] with members of the Indiana National Guard stationed in Washington, D.C., and thanked them for their service. He expressed appreciation for their continued dedication to Indiana and the nation. Congresswoman Victoria Spartz spoke [] at a Joint Economic Committee Republicans hearing on health care costs, highlighting how government incentives and barriers have driven consolidation and rising prices. Congressman Jefferson Shreve led [] a letter urging the State Department to pursue a coordinated U.S. strategy to counter the growing influence of China and Russia in Latin America. He emphasized strengthening partnerships with reform-minded democratic nations to protect U.S. interests and democratic values. Congressman Mark Messmer requested [] that the U.S. Navy name a future warship after Evansville, honoring the city’s long history of supporting American troops. He said Evansville’s legacy makes it deserving of the honor of the USS Evansville. Congresswoman Erin Houchin announced [] service academy nominations for outstanding students from Indiana’s 9th District. The nominees were selected through a competitive process recognizing academic excellence, leadership, and a commitment to serving the nation. If this administration really wanted to crack down on violence-inciting speech, what about Lauren Boebert?
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001G_tOoWrTwM9HGGMjAn2EZSHHVXd-sky1noA4h4qQEPHMP_JooqDxseSMMYxaRGbaJ0_az6-N80U95QBDF_kzKf4bTMiPxzhp8Um_oeLGmTYhh8zvd8cfNfowl8-TZt7d-hHgZZUnouLM7ctjrKr9juFEmJ_9_svAKt2Jd8FqvAxlTiZ8yZqA7rxCb3rIIArsG3NcjhmqP50nO8fVTlJRwQT7xfgftP20&c=7hNwY3r1tRNaBAD5gyUYPiakQMCFvOy2r-uxu1We90LGRJmt-RXEmA==&ch=ZNIBOZ_kXovJyUTWB6ll-dCmxC1XRWVZ25icEaKdTApEzsDACBBMWA==)

|

Peter,
The Trump administration is cracking down on free speech under the notion that Democrats are driving violence for simply calling the administration what it is: fascist.
That's one of the hallmarks of fascism. The meaning of words and language are inverted to manipulate people's understanding. If this administration really wanted to crack down on violence-inciting speech, what about Lauren Boebert?
I'm talking about the Boebert who once bragged "I am the militia," vowed to bring a gun to work in Congress, was implicated in leading reconnaissance tours before January 6th and Tweeted the location of the US Speaker on that day to a bloodthirsty mob. And last year, on Steve Bannon's "War Room," she called for the creation of a Taliban-like "holy army" to "save Colorado from the left."
---

While Trump pressures ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel over harmless free speech and Attorney General Pam Bondi threatens to prosecute so-called left-wing "hate speech," Republicans like Boebert openly glorify political violence.
This is GOP hypocrisy. They want free speech for themselves--but silence, intimidation, and even violence for anyone who dares to oppose them.
Lauren Boebert is extreme. If she wins reelection, she'll only have a bigger platform to mobilize the extremists she courts.

clean_body: If this administration really wanted to crack down on violence-inciting speech, what about Lauren Boebert? | | | | | == | Peter, The Trump administration is cracking down on free speech under the notion that Democrats are driving violence for simply calling the administration what it is: fascist. That's one of the hallmarks of fascism. The meaning of words and language are inverted to manipulate people's understanding. If this administration really wanted to crack down on violence-inciting speech, what about Lauren Boebert? I'm talking about the Boebert who once bragged "I am the militia," vowed to bring a gun to work in Congress, was implicated in leading reconnaissance tours before January 6th and Tweeted the location of the US Speaker on that day to a bloodthirsty mob. And last year, on Steve Bannon's "War Room," she called for the creation of a Taliban-like "holy army" to "save Colorado from the left." | == While Trump pressures ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel over harmless free speech and Attorney General Pam Bondi threatens to prosecute so-called left-wing "hate speech," Republicans like Boebert openly glorify political violence. This is GOP hypocrisy. They want free speech for themselves--but silence, intimidation, and even violence for anyone who dares to oppose them. Lauren Boebert is extreme. If she wins reelection, she'll only have a bigger platform to mobilize the extremists she courts. I am the Democrat running to defeat Lauren Boebert and I need you in this fight. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == We can't let their hypocrisy--and their violence--destroy our nation. Trisha Calvarese Democrat for Colorado's 4th | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

name: Team WisGOP
subject: WisGOP Update 12.19.2025
body:
No images? [Click here](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-e-aotkdt-didthutlhu-ji/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-r/) Patriot,This holiday season, we want to wish you a Merry Christmas and extend our gratitude to all of you. We want to say thank you to everyone who has volunteered, voted, donated, and done so much to help elect conservative leaders and spread our message!It has been a great year for conservative politics and policies that Wisconsinites can be proud of! From bringing down costs for families to securing our border, we have seen a lot of wins for our state and our country! Merry Christmas and Happy New Year!-Team WisGOP **The Gift of Good Policies** President Trump's second term has been a gift to Wisconsin and the nation as a whole. We have seen campaign promises delivered everyday to make our country safer and more affordable for everyone.**Wins for Wisconsin** Wisconsin has been particularly lucky to have President Trump in office this year, with a strong economic turnaround taking hold in the Badger State!
* Wisconsin saw fewer unemployment claims in [September](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-y/) of this year than any other month ever recorded in state history!
* Nearly [200,000](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-j/) bar and restaurant workers in our state no longer have to pay taxes on their tips! Now Wisconsin workers get to keep their money in their communities instead of sending it to Washington!
* Education, healthcare, and tourism have [boomed](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-t/) in Wisconsin since President Trump took office, adding thousands of jobs to our economy!
* Gas in our state [costs](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-i/) just $2.51 a gallon instead of $4.92 when Biden was in office!

**Gas Prices Hit Record Lows** President Trump is delivering on his promise to make America affordable again by bringing down gas prices, which hit [record highs](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-d/) under President Biden. Lower gas and energy prices mean lower costs for businesses, thus lowering prices for all goods!
* Average gas [prices](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-h/) hit $2.51 in Wisconsin and $2.79 nationwide. That's the lowest level in over four years, and prices are trending lower still!
* Americans are on track to [spend less](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-k/) of their disposable income on gas than any point in the last two decades.
* President Biden's term in office saw the [highest](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-u/) average gas prices for any American Presidency ever. The average driver spent an extra [$548](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-o/) a year on more expensive gas during his term, and households spent an extra [$850](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-b/) a year in energy costs under Biden.

**Making America Safe Again** Illegal crossings at the border are down to historic [lows](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-n/) while drug seizures are [up](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-a/). Homicides in major American cities have declined as President Trump delivers his promise to Make America Safe Again!
* Alongside [2.2 million](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-f/) deportations and departures of illegal migrants, over [60,000](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-z/) missing children allowed to cross without a sponsor have been found. Many of them have been saved from being trafficked, forced into labor, and mistreated.
* The Southern Border is silent as US Border Patrol has set a [record low](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-v/) number of encounters for the start of a fiscal year. November also marked the 7th consecutive month with zero releases of illegal migrants into the U.S.
* DHS reports that [54,947](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-e/) pounds of drugs were seized nationwide in November, a 33% increase from October.
* The first half of this year saw a [17%](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-g/) decrease in homicide across 30 American cities and other crimes including carjackings, aggravated assault, and many others have dropped dramatically!

**Take Action!****We need Letter to the Editor Signers!**
Become a Letter to the Editor (LTE) signer and help get the Republican message out to every community across the state. LTE volunteers are critical to continuing to advance our Republican values. Our goal is to have groups in every county who submit LTEs regularly. These groups will have resources provided from our team, including talking points, draft LTEs, and submission instructions. If you have any questions, please reach out to our Communications Director, Anika Rickard, at [arickard@wisgop.org](mailto:arickard@wisgop.org). [BECOME A LTE SIGNER!](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-w/) **Become a Poll Observer!**
Missed the poll worker deadline? Don't worry, there's still time to become a poll observer. Sign up below! [BECOME A POLL OBSERVER!](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-yd/)
# **Worth the Read**
[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-yh/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-yk/)
# **Top Shareable Content**
[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-yu/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jl/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jr/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jy/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jj/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-l-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jt/) | [ Share ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-fb-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jd/) | [ Tweet ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-tw-aotkdt-didthutlhu-jh/) | [ Forward ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.forwardtomyfriend.com/y-didthutlhu-931243C5-aotkdt-l-jk)
---|---|---

Johnson Street | Madison, WI 53703 [Preferences](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.updatemyprofile.com/y-aotkdt-931243C5-didthutlhu-ju) | [Unsubscribe](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail19.com/t/y-u-aotkdt-didthutlhu-tl/)

| | Hi Fellow Conservative, We wanted to quickly share something Senator Ted Cruz said recently on _Life, Liberty & Levin_ that is very important. He talked about President Trump's role as commander in chief and emphasized that going after narco-terrorists is a core responsibility of his job- as protecting American lives is vital to us all. _Senator Cruz said that President Trump is faithfully carrying out his solemn duty to protect the country and save American lives. President Trump is doing the right thing. Period!_ _Of course_ , almost all patriotic Americans agree with Senator Cruz and President Trump on this. But it is truly concerning that we have Democrats who are openly advocating to protect the lives of terrorists and working to stop our military from conducting strikes against narco-terrorists in international waters- it's insane, and extremely worrisome... **_That 's why we must continue to fight back against the radical Democrats and raise as much money as we possibly can to defeat them in 2026 and beyond. They're dangerous, and winning is our only option._** [**Agree? # Saturday, December 20, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1940, _Captain America Comics #1_ hit newsstands, featuring the now-iconic cover of Captain America punching Adolf Hitler. Released nearly a year before the U.S. entered World War II, the comic sold close to a million copies and helped cement the Golden Age of comic books. [See the cover here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd0Qw_i8obuMut93SGsPYnl0MFpBYeftV8grbHR0dHX36vu7zHdlz2P7jQaascQ50ZU-lUJoTOcel8JkLpRxiPF850BWvGRS0UoMENTtk_5qbypmVCKiuyOi-6wCGbuir3ah1Uf1gdYOwQEdqpGFQoAekdnVjTRWb95sGUFqrpmlvrdX16ADHHvu6xlE0ihyXmG7fkUPZafWv-KR0s_GovNlLObOo_wmYBEy5JAwbpSsm7_J7ImACBUrApOGTBUwxSEdWgpJ6bJS0eBbkIPGgxBblgT-U5c5ZOloeRZ6PYHcOm9y5eEoSg5ML2Xi8OwySbQwCpioZd17sPeeuF43aDmT_oSx_Uvs08BsAAP__egiNJA).

The CEO of _The Flyover,_ Cole Strehlow, is hosting today’s _Flyover Podcast_ for the first time _._ Yes, the man who oversees _The Flyover_ operation is stepping up to the microphone for a rare, audio-only episode covering today’s news that you won’t want to miss. [Tune in here!](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMFuqzAURL8GdiB8bTAsvHh6LarUpMqqTbNBDr4kbh2M7JtY9OurtJ_Q1SxmNEc6b9thq62z8-lFX1DR5IcZU3RIhGEIaIwlskskDHH2flnjjMmnASqoCwYFVIPR1q2DyI2aEAF5jorJpgFom1bmZyVaBg3HEZGjqKWWvDuKSY61qLmY6ja36v7GACrGKiHqsh4Z7wzo7j7mustEhcaSD-WHtzOdcXKrv2EoR3_JnToTLTHj_zLoM-hTSuXqr3Q94r3PoF-cXp2NlPH-Nx52m8fX-L6n5537vzt8PfVhL4vNcgjDZzUXzMguD8osyTpnYyMzUZ0u2rofHv1J0k3BdwAAAP__lkd7YA)

As someone who cares deeply about sharing God’s Word and standing with persecuted believers, from today's sponsors, [OneHope](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL_unDAQhJ_GdD-E14ChcBGJ0F2KNCmRgxfYyH-QvXfo3j4iujdI-2nm02h-PZaHJU9x_2EDGt7SEvEqHpkxLxmdI2Y6C2MuMaXzXSJe6Vqgge5Lwhc0i7Pk30tbObMhAqoKjdR9DzD0g64OM0D3GzapRidbp9vR2k51iL3uh9Guo67I3DYJ0EjZtG1Xd-udBjtqq5Wyo2gbdMQp138SRT5w8-_0wlyvKVTeHMxnEeqbgFnAvNOL4l6niEc6sY7IAubT7ihgLmyju4iPSDtmskLNTw5LSc-8olDTxyugv3FAR88g1ITBkv_A1YbT0h6Fmqb5J3Ry-B58lY07L_KeSq9F2-x34d86_q9LXwb-BgAA___mLoyu), you have an urgent opportunity today to bring hope and comfort to Christians in Nigeria who are suffering unimaginable violence for their faith.

## Reddit Tipster Led Police to Brown Shooter
Police say information [from a Reddit tipster](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL8O2yAQxp8Gb47gcII9MHRhS9eOFglHchUGCy5BfvvK7SN0uVs-fX9-v-7r3VOi_PrpN7Qcy5qxt4TMWNeKIRAz7Y2xtlzKfrSMvfQVJFxHBSPINXhKxzoNwUZEQD2gVeZ2A5hvsxnedvJmjirqRwwyLmoO2k-zDLD4p5TxgQPZ000BSKXkNF0v16fSSwC_GG-09ouYJAbiUi-_C2V-Y0xH-WK9PMs2JPtm3pvQPwQ4Aa73fvk0zyX44xQIcI1LPQS4c9n5PFPJAtwZK8ApOM8iwD1q6Xn8ZPpibcTH2N6lMOXX2D5txyeP_4iMe2k8bsQC3GzmSUuppTQC3FBt2DulRO1mxCRfm6f0tyf_F9yvhT8BAAD__5lDjeU) who had a strange encounter with another man on a sidewalk outside Brown University provided the key to finding the man who killed two students at the school and later an MIT professor. The suspect, 48-year-old Claudio Manuel Neves Valente, a former Brown student, was found dead by self-inflicted gunshot wound Thursday night in a storage unit in Salem, New Hampshire. Investigators say the breakthrough in the Brown University shooting came from an anonymous man dubbed “John,” who [noticed a stranger acting oddly](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFutDAQhJ8GOk722hhcuPib6-5vUyKDl2Mjn43szSHePiLKG6QcjTSa7_t4TA9PkdLzv3-h4zVPCY8akRnLVDAEYqa9Mpaact7PmvDIxwQC-k5CB2IKnuI56Ta4FRFQtejkYAzAaMah3RzOXvdSDgPMVnhjEIQVGoX0Zhl9EC25a00CCCmF1v2tX6SyAbwd_KCUt40WGIhzuX1mSrzhGs_8xnJb8quNbmPea6P-NXBv4O73C-CqrlCYlogN3OeSj9TVLWem9Oy-Er2xVOKzO4gT1tr9YnYopcZZjaBHBAEqLGG14MGiWcysRFtc2A-KkaoZGi2eL0_x5wr_yd_bwXcAAAD__1YVhPo) on campus and then posted about it on Reddit. After other Reddit users urged him to call police, John relayed a key detail: a Nissan with Florida plates that he thought was a rental. The tip and surveillance video, along with the use of license-plate reader technology, [led investigators to a car rental agency](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM1urSAURp8GZxjY4N-AwU1unJ1OmqZDg7JVGgQDu8f49s3pK3T8JV_WWp-P6WF98HF7swcaWtMU8SoBiTBPGZ3zRP4shLnElM67RLzSNYGAhkvgICZnfbgnXTmzIgKqCo3s2hagb_uu2s2qhk6qTq69mnUrWjHbdoBhcI2Ye-2WypvXmwQQUgqtm7pZpBoc2KGznVJ2YFqg85Ry_ZV8pB3XcKcn5npJRxXMTnQWpv4xGBmMdl5eBvWWXjOD8eOdwRjx4g7J-lA4Hpg35HNOV-SHJ172lMjHjZfvcuJC3K6EmcFYKOWbqdE7pv5L6JsGhG6qbNx5-RB8aTumxXZYH35Z6E8BnwZ-AgAA__9DmIS8) in Massachusetts, and ultimately to the Salem storage facility.
## Sony Buys _Peanuts_ for $457 Million
Sony has agreed to pay $457 million to double its stake in Peanuts Holdings LLC, giving it [80% ownership of Charlie Brown](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuhDAQBNCvMR0ne23MUbhIQ3dpU6IFL3eOjI3szSH-PiK_kGaakd5I8_WYHhhiSM9P3MjxmqdER43ETGUq5H1gDntlKjXlvJ810ZGPCSR0rYIW5OQxxHMyjXcrEZBuyKneWoC7vffNy8kBtKb7LAeJ1NOAcl7AWGtmknaxaxPcpSkAqZQ0prt1i9KDBxx67LXGQRhJPnAut-8cEr9ojWd-U7kteWuiezHvVegPAaOA0RP6GBJdpYDxogWMCgSMO2H64drOBZNva45XpLP1hLFVoK3ttLJWwNgU5_cjxBiq7YWRzw1D_Jvjf330dvAbAAD__xBBeL4), Snoopy and the whole _Peanuts_ cartoon gang created by cartoonist Charles Schulz. The Japanese entertainment giant is buying the 40% stake it doesn't own from [Canadian entertainment company WildBrain,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkEGupCAYhE-DOwz8iD4XLPql465n04tZGhp-lQmCAbqJt584c4S3rUpVqr7fj_mhnXdh_aV3VGWJc8CaPZaCaU5orSvFHblgyiHG48wBa6wzMJCUAwU2W-38OXeNVQsioGhQ8aHvAb76r6HZlOWvl7QaeyNkJ0dgOBphUCyG9ZIJ2zh1tXEAxjnrOtlKw8VoQY-DHoTQI-kYWldiav9EF8qGiz_jB1Nr4t54tZVyZCJuBCYCU6213XO4PAITBvrOBKY9BjwJTCbuhw4OLy3HcNI16Vem_79RHSwtG9IDdXiXTFcdVuoC7SSju_PexUAtak9g0onebvx5H8o3EVM0zhJx_744Ys1PTEeTlD3qFcr9QDq27tr5f4vLjzB_FPwNAAD__wZKkxg) which also owns the Teletubbies and Strawberry Shortcake brands. On this day in 1940, Captain America Comics #1 hit newsstands, featuring the now-iconic cover of Captain America punching Adolf Hitler. Released nearly a year before the U.S. entered World War II, the comic sold close to a million copies and helped cement the Golden Age of comic books. . As someone who cares deeply about sharing God’s Word and standing with persecuted believers, from today's sponsors, , you have an urgent opportunity today to bring hope and comfort to Christians in Nigeria who are suffering unimaginable violence for their faith. | Reddit Tipster Led Police to Brown Shooter Police say information who had a strange encounter with another man on a sidewalk outside Brown University provided the key to finding the man who killed two students at the school and later an MIT professor. The suspect, 48-year-old Claudio Manuel Neves Valente, a former Brown student, was found dead by self-inflicted gunshot wound Thursday night in a storage unit in Salem, New Hampshire. Investigators say the breakthrough in the Brown University shooting came from an anonymous man dubbed “John,” who on campus and then posted about it on Reddit. After other Reddit users urged him to call police, John relayed a key detail: a Nissan with Florida plates that he thought was a rental. The tip and surveillance video, along with the use of license-plate reader technology, in Massachusetts, and ultimately to the Salem storage facility. Sony Buys Peanuts for $457 Million Sony has agreed to pay $457 million to double its stake in Peanuts Holdings LLC, giving it , Snoopy and the whole Peanuts cartoon gang created by cartoonist Charles Schulz. The Japanese entertainment giant is buying the 40% stake it doesn't own from which also owns the Teletubbies and Strawberry Shortcake brands. The deal announced Friday values the Peanuts business at more than $1 billion, with the Schulz family retaining its current 20% stake. In addition to the Peanuts TV shows and specials on Apple+ TV, the brand encompasses , as well as amusement park attractions, cultural events, social events, social media, and comic strips in all formats. List: Most Valuable College Sports Programs The University of Texas at Austin athletic program is the most valuable in the U.S., topping a recent report of athletic programs, which in total are valued at a combined $51.22 billion, or 13% more than last year. The rankings covered NCAA Football Bowl Subdivision schools and analyzed factors including conference affiliation, estimated NIL spending, school subsidies, alumni totals, and other drivers of revenue growth. Top-ranked Texas has a $1.48 billion valuation and generated about $332 million in revenue, up 23% from last year, with donors providing the largest share at $137 million. By comparison, No. 75 University of Nevada, Las Vegas, carried a $190 million valuation and generated $61 million in revenue. ### Thin construction, locked-in owners, and a market with no exits.
| | [The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/@theangrydem)
---
| Dec 20| | | ∙| | Preview

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=182103001&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTAzMDAxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NjIzMjcxNCwiZXhwIjoxNzY4ODI0NzE0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzE1OTI1OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JAozV8KIdTrjnBRACPJprktVsoXcrwXTLdpBNQfPVpk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mattdiemer/p/the-ohio-housing-problem-is-worse?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Housing affordability did not collapse because people suddenly got greedy.
It did not collapse because young people buy lattes.
It did not collapse because builders forgot how to build houses.
It collapsed because we stacked policy decisions on top of human incentives and then acted shocked by the outcome.
I sat down with [Jasson Farrier](https://open.substack.com/users/308847628-jasson-farrier?utm_source=mentions) , who spends his days buried in housing data across Ohio and the broader Midwest, to cut through the narratives and talk about what is actually happening. Not the vibes. Not the headlines. The mechanics.
Find **Jasson Farrier** at:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d992fb69-55e1-4060-bf38-9becd6b2ea25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

| The Collagen Con | Did you know that [magnesium is vital](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=c0495266836e53c2ca07f275fa892fd50b574720a7b0bec56b4ee5658ada51744883bf10eb6ac41379a4854c3c142492508ea7a2d2d59bc1) for human health?
It's involved in **more than 300** metabolic processes...
And if you're not getting enough magnesium, it could be hurting _your sleep_ , _your energy levels_ , and even _your ability to lose weight_.
Now there are two big problems when it comes to magnesium...
**Problem #1:** Most Americans are deficient. We're talking like 80% don't get enough.
This is a huge reason why so many people are suffering from poor sleep, low energy, and obesity.
It's because our magnesium levels are too low. We're driving on fumes. And we're sputtering out long before the day is over.
How can you move forward if your tank is empty? You can't.
**Problem #2:** Most magnesium supplements can't solve the problem.
Why? Because they contain only one or two of the cheapest, lowest quality forms of magnesium...
The kinds that your body can't really use and could be potentially harmful to you. Crazy, right?
So what should you do...?
**THE SOLUTION:** You get a full-spectrum magnesium supplement like Organixx Magnesium 7.
When I say "full spectrum," I mean it has **seven different high quality forms of magnesium** that will __infuse__ and __energize__ your body with this vital mineral.
As a result, your sleep will be better, your metabolism will be higher, and it will be easier for you to burn off the fat in those trouble areas.
You'll also be getting **vital support** to maintain blood sugar and BP in normal ranges. (Yes, magnesium is involved in those processes, too.)
Can just one supplement really do all of this? **YES, absolutely.**
But I _DO NOT_ expect you to take my word for it. And so...
Thanks to the special relationship I have with Organixx, I'm able to offer you [this "super magnesium" with ALL 7 forms of magnesium for better sleep, more energy & fat burning](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=c0495266836e53c2ca07f275fa892fd50b574720a7b0bec56b4ee5658ada51744883bf10eb6ac41379a4854c3c142492508ea7a2d2d59bc1) – no subscriptions or strings attached.
**Why are they doing this?**
It's simple...
They want to prove to you that Magnesium 7 is one of the best magnesium supplements on the market for supporting...

* **Improved sleep**
* **Higher metabolism**
* **An elevated mood**
* **And a feeling of calmness**

They also know the people on my list are serious about their health, so that's why they agreed to give you a **[Special Flash Sale](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=c0495266836e53c2ca07f275fa892fd50b574720a7b0bec56b4ee5658ada51744883bf10eb6ac41379a4854c3c142492508ea7a2d2d59bc1)** with your order today.
Supplies are limited, so I'd hurry if you're interested.

# A Winter Update from Your National Committeeman & Committeewoman
---
Dear Republican Friends,
The Christmas season is always a special time, and this year in Washington was no exception. Over the past few weeks, we had the chance to take part in Christmas gatherings across the city, attend trainings and meetings, reconnect with old friends, meet new ones, and, of course, keep fighting for Massachusetts.
The Political, Media, and Data Trainings held by the Republican National Committee were top-tier and focused on making an immediate impact. One particularly memorable evening was a Christmas reception at Vice President JD Vance's residence. How fortunate we are to be part of this moment in our country's history! As with all the events, the best time was spent sharing stories and celebrating the good work you all do each and every day here at home.
As the year winds down, these moments are a welcome pause and a good reminder of why we do this work, and how very important you are in the fight for the future of Massachusetts.
THANK YOU!
Cheers to 2026,
---
| | Janet R. Fogarty MA RNC National Committeewoman MA GOP State Committeewoman
---
| | Brad Wyatt MA RNC National Committeeman

Updates from Washington
> DEMOCRATS ARE TRYING TO SHIFT THE BLAME FOR OBAMACARE PREMIUMS
> Democrats are the ones who voted to expand Obamacare subsidies when they controlled Congress in 2022 and to set the expiration date for those subsidies at the end of this year. This is a crisis of Democrats' own making, which they are now trying to blame Republicans for.
* Democrats are once again blaming Republicans for problems [they created](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup7SqJ_uUm2kEIeK2o1SvbF_S5Raigxjdcgm0tM-DlV_K5_ae-VcPMYvUlfPRcIDX12InDK37qhvCtS97UKM2jLWTb6QwM-Y98m_LvQ1-CrlF0SfUWdhGjXOsQhVN3TPxWNDxQBctElx8K72BJdpUNMD5wSpxgnzkC7RU2wsXXJ5uARlIjzZhc4OvCjr7C3exEYH7ri9c9dMGPHheqE-I1WWcMoD526HlekzMUxzQbsTEsXIljMICuvw==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==).
* When Democrats were in control of Congress under Joe Biden, they created new Obamacare subsidies and [set them to expire](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup9Ro_risqfLa73rgjjgkpJjWkpyxFrL6lSuSi-vREjnifGe43wDiqKxeEtyWg5VW6c-oP4a9V65S_f12CK50ZXFpAM4aqp69galsAistiTOF-dWkradtvoAGTuAWFWbkpJidLinK5xIPoZyVpdqnQU6uKApMQIHoUBqKBniUl_8JYClfygsE5mpCyZF609Hzf_qZRncKLBFSERDZLhLuIwYgYKTeidrsJxDqrQwDgj8Li8V1inI14tw==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) at the end of 2025 without a single Republican vote.
* These extended Obamacare subsidies were put into Biden's Inflation "[Reduction](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupJ7uGhsB_5yEH5YraVNgw3nGQuqSWPwqbfn2OFFiYeMSQfrS2CYG610Ws9SV17Fid6Ng_W1qIa_5scoddDEgfNWGfIS45j27VfqZCTCvwyngtbTZRN53_WQo9n2b2osUQ8lm6yRGbdt72g1CDH8btx9MJ2pLjkiC3zizXDS3UGqjznB3v9c-BDMx-aDguuRY8iR2yPrsh6v47HKVadlBRGZC9D8b6EUI4MNR5hk6FmAEhvUhl0mKUEQ==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==)" Act and Democrats' multi-trillion dollar stimulus that caused prices to skyrocket and wrecked the U.S. economy.
* This is a crisis of the Democrats own making.
* [The text of the "Inflation Reduction Act"](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup0YAfD_XrrfWT9sctAo6wjlxqftFzqvSSfyIgh0QS85EpqR7Qhu31BQIWzv7M2-AFqtviMHHEl-7T_8R3k1W19Q2WHy_7O7tn4SPaUyomQIeDmspvmub5qOyMBHeq3Y1BFREDWzDPU7gdVTZPxB-f4Ok3sBxIF3icLaH8PVWG6Pd2itZ1e53z40azdtD_h3TOqsxvMtalpcbY7iTTQLpNmUfJeTsjlHkY6exKU9ZaAJ0-ifAfSiLOiA==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) - which Democrats [wrote](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupczFmfDmWSYBky-wPFW8ioqzums1drscPgsMwvxYE9Z245vsZPatBaR1chmHczYXVXMXDxw3A8Nn82q2YpXV5udTsLi5pS1iJHWvP95JJqikINA7jzEi86YdvqLYPtsJzP1kTbdZQ4zE0mgo3pR4q_yozztec27dKUjsf2ItCAvUaBVbMHylVZGHeHgLqKNw5yjF9t1whFlCsCVH2bjrXJCz-cpYUNfRIweqIqD7SDGeasiluN_XjQw==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) and voted for - created this expiration date.
* Not a [single Republican](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup9_YlsIKppVfvSihEzaSv1NovAMwpXSR_n0_pdQA4bwfjePIfqkOJo4YvLIysfVsfPqzTc1VzFAIB0MDlULGHqe_AI9PuwRjFsv-v1MJ9ov8nOZGIkWtNGxVegpxNIY4Q4RpyZOtsfEGKLkxP6YsVEbTKhlP8v5iR1w-FWppt73fUMvAdcYadtWHuNGrwZMhoMVErc09RBqbQpTynAMpu9BZvpIufOsTWr-9hnJOZp6keXGq1tiM4lg==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) voted for this bill; it passed with only Democrat votes.
* These subsidies were originally created as part of Biden's wasteful stimulus package - the so-called "[American Rescue Plan](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupyTH8DSzz4-u6TLMYl8HCKH1MIc27i56TPMO8jfIeJrLb7RDlIuPgdaZDYd8Qxm9_oZFB6Mz08qZD-WkT6DIbPeBw-L-ulIgwWiJrnsyjmiex74wYL48uxjp_EEclmdG9_Epx3S0yTVKhHIAUyRzk3Wq7Cq5sMiR89wb1gHfe--z-slGcVwXrh5jwDRzJuKMoFGiEZhTmE9uKNCb5-tLB_5j7iy5PldW3w5180AFiZJOcwerwjyfgrA==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==)" - which also was [written](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup2tXdChBOk6GY6pM6FJTQTcT37FTfV_DGznvkdx5PFSM-0fD_mJycHf0CGCHvsId9bm-7KXy_udGkoLFRk0s5cno6gseR52TFP2g7Guimn4_VA5Agu3sevcOWfKQUGlzohB7mmyNJmu6atTL2_Bk0OTdPHVZpe_Jb16ukqWTrIDQzNqbn2j5hAod3gtNcKNF5js13O_4ElsieANEjcaqiHW7svbbGqReIRzITnOhdBmWk3aDX4ZwKog==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) and passed by Democrats with no [Republican votes](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupsz8v8q4JINeoXMpoweUTynICqSC5W4EhZLwlUvJYiYC_vzC1Lwo8dauBVtawfSZ6odtcRZiI47ZuuUMNeFrEZYTlBOqgxKpg4mZO4_oSaNvahcYrhn8UOJAQcGvMpgBnhLXTNmF9PnWU2m638PFD__ADEnQaR2t6sIyXEw43EeMTdAx__m_0CpDwFg-xKlJvb_tnIqxbfuqC5z0TNiiYj4WY77blSIHY32cZB8oWl7TBUCbDnz0iXw==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==).
* Democrats are now blaming Republicans for this and actively [refusing](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAup3B6JiTDz3nrJuINRdQ12xPNh6HpdWXGZj3tEEV0WcIwMvaMuH7VlCK5CAmKsDA2WtPf8w4b6C_H5PoNEPxOehMIu4qSlGSGSRt-db6Er5F7tHZUZCJCrMeR4SGaQAvwvNQEjvZsnBYwnm-9YPi89exoxCyYVk2k4jDNXoJOxROuG-nWiDWvlgknJV5Y9ZsKpHLLdOtXIsjV3dcSj2j4T9AAlDVlQ1YJoYTajBoQi_pWQB8N6Iy1AJw==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) to work with Republicans.

* Cumulatively, energy costs under 4 years of Biden were more than $5,200 per [American](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupQ06LsPROf2djFkfoMpqyB1Dknm8cB1AJ2Ku0lpTeh360IPwCvHgRXUE6PxK9WG-Ersa5f-Jt2pwYsNO9H8kswVC0My4ZxLMkokWWa7FouCEiapIq_V6mQx1P6l0Xeelw28HiF83804FFra7vCG1dsMKLzK0xFciyw5nBImFZ2ndXpq6BcWmOaGVJnIz3LIv2ol_xRzYQAWlj-SLRoEpQt6ridxWe4LkvX9T1vOboln3CswLdDc0QHg==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) household.
* High gas prices are one and the same with the Biden presidency.
* Americans are on track to spend the [lowest amount](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupn-05nCNkon81Q8A6gbvkI04a1L7_g-0umTQVCJsz9T1HJdrWckFEj23oJswEwvvg_B7jXZmOoUjo4JbhWyxGsoGXDD3sxoGJdWug7kZN5AhXkro2fRB3znYSy5v_UTtBTIT1-EkpDfFLiGpw5Ap6hO3mzZIOqnAUOnDYto_t5Tnr_NNGmQg94odEePps30pie6hQrIF9NEPyI1ZeiizCGzqp1YvhaucG2MOb1G9S281vpN1k3BLTLQ==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) of their disposable income on gas in the last two decades under the Trump administration.
* Joe Biden and Democrats' reckless spending and anti-energy policies drove inflation and prices through the roof and crushed working families.
* President Trump won't stop providing relief for ALL Americans, and things are finally moving in the right direction.

>
>
> PRESIDENT TRUMP PROVIDES SUPPORT TO AMERICAN FARMERS
> President Trump is providing much needed relief to American farmers who are struggling after four years of Biden abandoning them.
* President Trump [announced](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupq-2YO4GHzsoFla_Qp9pYDPWB2VWfq_8M17pClwPU1cDpXlviF46w0sQ8M1kt5MgO1KpR2tMXn7MoMUB9gSIjBG_3xY05p_iqlCd6ca6V2Sz1biYNdjgui_Kjyw-87dW2-6eDT2nUHxDet0iwHkiTvZABhQrhqwBsV47HmKUDTGBd-PVFcLFvTwS2hczSOTq60KkJUqg3Zjg_VAofPl-4iS2q7U0hqp9ilWebjHppJsHWRFAiHetiog==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) he is providing $12 billion in relief to American farmers to deal with temporary disruptions in agricultural markets.
* American farmers are still facing the consequences of four years of disastrous Biden administration policies.
* These payments will help farmers until historic investments from the Working Families Tax Cuts and the President's trade deals go into effect.
* Of the [$12 billion](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupWFbf7zoNJWVNKhCmg83-DM8b30dhAXULLBT9K--Z_drspc3_tHNzQeKJVXpsyc9Rev6vCuuYW5cSO09wUs_-LqizLb-V2glQFWzM3EF159qNnZunBxsbB0SsnxwVivsSAgtm9HdrfLfl55sGtjzl3nCF3fYbBJfxFiv_pbHvhv_3KUNu0H4MT_4-1t65NDFwA9D8M7cBdI4sAQUlrs0B-zR8OQsXlWmPB42TiJfCdYtUJiHah91WVQ==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) provided, up to $11 billion will be used for the Farmer Bridge Assistance (FBA) Program, which provides relief to U.S. crop farmers.
* FBA will provide relief to farmers from market disruptions, higher input costs, and foreign competitors engaging in unfair trade practices that prevent American farmers from exporting more of their products.
* The remaining $1 billion will be reserved for commodities not covered in the FBA Program.
* President Trump is ensuring our great farmers can see quick relief while our new trade deals take effect.
* President Trump is fighting to bring down costs for farmers, reverse the effects of Bidenflation, and stop foreign countries from cheating in trade.
* Maximizing farm production in the United States is a big part of how we will bring down grocery prices for American families.
* [President Trump](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001qJ-pF2Y_HApjJWX0Z00G5eKKU-C1KqjoWJOAK1UMIT0x_XT3V9LwR_PQkGi_pAupZykUZ6zmIiH5bfdeAKcu3vKBgPZQQwqRJfQyDsgdwqy-hrFn3EA9_u847B1XYWpZbpRma6KhHkWLbrpkM3PnGfYJpl_ukVZ5asAn142aMCAiCgohseoAre5WLJRu2jR_qx6_ySpK05zolEiM9BwAG9cW1glAXNYG8ZLTvtRWprC6LWjoyjp8NMt9bk4lIrKaLYbESiHaTmtKzEdudBXuSJYzhsttdFZnLZ6-ncANSi3brGdfwK3k4g==&c=hG1Zo1_I3CxhtfIfgjqajpkRyVT7lhGZilGIPluXah-nRVGNOpTRQQ==&ch=y7iOWNCCQiU4b8m38TOePI8FjMwt7AojVX8sVlguhbFETHfyg1b0-w==) : "Farmers are an indispensable national asset, part of the backbone of America...I've always felt it so strongly. They are so important."

body:
A Texas judge ordered Attorney General Ken Paxton’s [divorce records](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGy2yAQRb8GdeuBFfCkQkUadS9tSs0aVjYJAg3wLOvvM3beH6TdnTl37rm_PpdPCjGk20_aeGrPNS-Jjxq5NS7LTs-Wkw-PXBwXdrn42vhJdUGJBhQCysVTiOeiOz9dpR9Vx5P6sBZ7VFJ298l4VmiNX81gDX0Y2TsppRrcOGh99aYL0wumEKVCqXp7MWq0bh1IS6PNqq3Qkn1ouVzand_xazzzg8vF5a2L0721vYr-h8BZ4Hwcx0XJ9ng9Bc5UWnCRBc71TD44aiGn173W7AI19nvhWgXObzL8_vI3hlw8lwrUWi6JT7hx4kIR_nCCf1IqfGuBby_wlSpTZA-0BQ93frGhcqLGsJewUTkFzlZZ0JrGcfQO3CoH0MgORi0dmL4fhn7A8cqmK5PfjxBjqPZDaHnbKMR34fZ_Mz0m_BsAAP__Q7Solg) to be unsealed Friday after Paxton and ex-wife, state Senator Angela Paxton, agreed to the legal challenge from a watchdog group and a coalition of media outlets.

See the game preview in today's _Roundup_. If you or someone you love has noticed uncomfortable changes when using the bathroom, today’s sponsor [Prostadine](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGuqzAQheHVmC7IHowhhYtIEV1e-0pkPONgyWBk5hKy-6tkC7c_Op_0_3-MDxdTXJ__3EKWz5DHlV57ImYq4-ZOzivGIxdPhXwuuDOdbh9BQntRcAE5oovpPeoK7STxqiqyqjMGGlBSVrO9trINaHqpne475Xs1KTCdmrDxvgumivZzpgCkAqkaU7fqanzonZatboM2QkvCyLnUPNOXD-mdDyq1z0uV7My87aK5CRgEDE_ireSdHcaVPgsBwxGRcr3Nm2gGF0JM0TGJ5j7lhG6jUwkwHFE095CWn52REOSdPLS3qljcXjGluJtOaPlcXExfmP-W67DwGwAA__9-zX72) shares important research that could help explain what those warning signs may really mean.

## Court Unseals Paxton's Divorce Records
A Texas judge ordered Attorney General Ken Paxton’s [divorce records](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGy2yAQRb8GdeuBFfCkQkUadS9tSs0aVjYJAg3wLOvvM3beH6TdnTl37rm_PpdPCjGk20_aeGrPNS-Jjxq5NS7LTs-Wkw-PXBwXdrn42vhJdUGJBhQCysVTiOeiOz9dpR9Vx5P6sBZ7VFJ298l4VmiNX81gDX0Y2TsppRrcOGh99aYL0wumEKVCqXp7MWq0bh1IS6PNqq3Qkn1ouVzand_xazzzg8vF5a2L0721vYr-h8BZ4Hwcx0XJ9ng9Bc5UWnCRBc71TD44aiGn173W7AI19nvhWgXObzL8_vI3hlw8lwrUWi6JT7hx4kIR_nCCf1IqfGuBby_wlSpTZA-0BQ93frGhcqLGsJewUTkFzlZZ0JrGcfQO3CoH0MgORi0dmL4fhn7A8cqmK5PfjxBjqPZDaHnbKMR34fZ_Mz0m_BsAAP__Q7Solg) to be unsealed Friday after Paxton and ex-wife, state Sen. Angela Paxton, agreed to the legal challenge from a watchdog group and a coalition of media outlets. Paxton is in the middle of a heated Republican primary race to unseat U.S. Sen. John Cornyn, with Cornyn’s campaign focusing on Paxton’s character and history of controversies. Angela Paxton filed for divorce in July, citing “[biblical grounds](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO8yAMB_CnIRsROIS0A8O3ZOu33hg54LToKFTANenbn5LrPcFttmT7r58_LtMFffDx-h_vZOq2pCnSWgLVSnl64FZTdP6ZsqVMNmVXKm1YJhDQcwkcxOTQh9ekGmdm4c6yISMHraEDKURzM3YeFouDc2RBke7sLJ1Swzz0cMZOnhpv9mMSQEgQstNtL8_aLidUolf9ojRTgpyvKbf1Rkf8El7pSbm16d4Ec6v1UVj3j8HIYFzXtV3SpnbGPsBg3EsG47HKPynyHxfHeKWAv91XLISBv7X8zW2ycY_Vh-CLHpgS1zv6cATXv73raeA7AAD__134gf8)” as well as “recent discoveries” on top of an extramarital affair exposed during Paxton’s 2023 impeachment proceedings. The Paxtons requested their divorce records be sealed shortly after filing, and Ken Paxton challenged the September motion from media outlets to unseal them as “intrusive,” before agreeing Thursday to accede to the demand.
## 'KFC Murders' 1983 Cold Case Officially Closed
A five-person homicide case that rocked East Texas in 1983 has finally been closed after a third suspect was identified in what are known as the “[KFC murders](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDHSpCAUhE-jGRY8AZ2AYBOzf9MNLYb3cKhFsYBR5_ZbzhU266C7v-r-8zP_2BDDtvy2K5l6-TRvdJZItVKed3vVtGE4UnaUyaWMpdJlywwcFBPAgM9oQ_zMskXz5PgQLRkxaA09CM7bl8FxBP5Q9qmUfnpEsoNDsEoM_ShHodtg7jIBwAVw0etOiYd2frSSK6m81I3khKGm3NUXffE-ftJBuXNpbaN51bqXpv_VwNTAdJ5n59Ml7xm3oYHplg1M3yj76x1b3xkpF-ZSROZsIVbeZSdXWUDaavCBkCEddmM5LEtps8H9DDGGoodG8mW1IX7p9f8-Owz8CwAA__9AeYMf).” [DNA evidence](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDHO4yAUhE-DOyx4xoALim3osu2WFoZHghabCFg7uf0qucJfjzTfzPfntt5cyum4_3Y7mv6KZT3wahl7x7o-3auXI6SzVI8VfamhdXy5tgKDmXKgwNbgUn6vYghmY2HhAxqupIQJOGPDwwimlJJh0Tih1qCV2nxUW-QQnN5mNSTzKeMAjAPjkxxnvkgftRNsFnMUkgiGIfVSx_7ALz7mdzmxjr7sQzaP3p-NTL8IWAL2uq7x2HyI1ycmYD9_CNhcvMsErAD6RlcbTceJrae766U2-jd6uv-rAWujvuRAvWtI0bVOv0gCdlpmPTFOwA7VhOeVck5NKiLYfXcpf9f0nzk8DfwPAAD__z5mhg8) from a victim’s clothing confirmed Devan Riggs as the final suspect in the murders after the Texas Rangers reopened the case in 2023. Riggs, together with Romeo Pinkerton and Darnell Hartsfield, who were convicted in 2007 and 2008, abducted their five victims from a KFC restaurant in Kilgore in September 1983 and murdered them “execution-style” on a remote site in Rusk County. Riggs has been dead for over a decade, meaning no further arrests will be made, and the case is officially closed.
## Texas A&M Set to Take on Hurricanes
Texas A&M takes to the field at home today to face the Miami Hurricanes in the [first College Football Playoff game](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuuszAQhJ8GOiJ7fYPCxd-ky9-eEtneJVnJxMhYubz9EbzCKUejmU8zP7f5Fjjz8_4_rOTbZynzk957ptaozlv4tPJEfpWaqFIqFfdGn7DPIMAMEgYQMwbO31n36KPASfbkpbMWFEgh-odPUWhQqLQZRxNSTDbiaHVUbkzoFt2zP8okgJAgpLIXIyebljFoYbRZtO20IORW6qU96MQv-VteVC-prH32j9a2vVP_Orh2cA3bMeCwDlEbp0wdXFcOKw9negjrkJZtUFJLRZgIopM4gREYk3MwTaNCA7avHrc358y7dZ0W9zVwPpntb0-9PPwGAAD__3Wsezo) ever played in College Station. The Aggies enter as slight favorites in the do-or-die matchup, with the winner advancing [in the playoff brackets](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOwyAURE-DOyz4xsQpKLZxl223tL7hY6MQYwEbJ7dfJVJOsPWMZubNz2W6YIhhW77xRqY-fJo2OkqkWilPOz5q2ly4p2wpk03ZlUoPLBMI6LkEDmJyGOJzUo0zs3Bn2ZCRJ62hAylEsxptB-lRWhSdsIMmBCfJinnuFCg52CaYV5gEEBKE7HTby7O2fkAletV7pZkS5EJNua0rvet9fKY75damWxPNWuteWPfFYGQwHsfRbhbxJTIYXzQMRp9SnTFGBiPmGmwkBuMHQp4ZjDbFSAvxj5PvEZ_Jez5ntFeqvNiV3G8kXmzKVHjG7Rq2pTTZuP0IMYaiT0yJ5YYhvqfV_x16N_AXAAD__2tGi6g) to play No. 2 Ohio State at the Cotton Bowl on New Year’s Eve. Texas A&M, ranked No. 7, is coming off its best regular season in over a decade: They’re 11-1, including 7-0 at Kyle Field, and are averaging over 36 points per game. Their defensive front has [logged 41 sacks](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2u6yAQxfHV4A4LJviroHiNu7z2ltYEBgcJBwtGsbP7q3gLtz_6_6Tzc1_uGFN8rf9xI8tnyMuLjpqImcqy48n55eM7F0eFXC6-Mp1YF1DQSQ0S1OIxps9iGm8fyk-6IauHvocbaKWapx0mpYPGcdBkjB9DR865aXI4kIFu9E2035gGUBqUvvVtp6fehRGN6kwXTC-MIh85l5afdPEhffKbSuvy1iT7ZN6ruP0TMAuYj-NoQz7rngvX70LA7HJKtJIMOfMDUxIwXx2Jm8R1jVQlE26yMnJtivX7EVOKtR-EUeuGMV0U_-2gt4XfAAAA__8DvHxY) so far this season, the most of any team in the country. All eyes will be on superstar running back Le’Veon Moss, who could play today after missing six straight games with an ankle injury. Angela Paxton, agreed to the legal challenge from a watchdog group and a coalition of media outlets. Paxton is in the middle of a heated Republican primary race to unseat U.S. Sen. John Cornyn, with Cornyn’s campaign focusing on Paxton’s character and history of controversies. Angela Paxton filed for divorce in July, citing “” as well as “recent discoveries” on top of an extramarital affair exposed during Paxton’s 2023 impeachment proceedings. The Paxtons requested their divorce records be sealed shortly after filing, and Ken Paxton challenged the September motion from media outlets to unseal them as “intrusive,” before agreeing Thursday to accede to the demand. 'KFC Murders' 1983 Cold Case Officially Closed A five-person homicide case that rocked East Texas in 1983 has finally been closed after a third suspect was identified in what are known as the “.” from a victim’s clothing confirmed Devan Riggs as the final suspect in the murders after the Texas Rangers reopened the case in 2023. Riggs, together with Romeo Pinkerton and Darnell Hartsfield, who were convicted in 2007 and 2008, abducted their five victims from a KFC restaurant in Kilgore in September 1983 and murdered them “execution-style” on a remote site in Rusk County. Riggs has been dead for over a decade, meaning no further arrests will be made, and the case is officially closed. Texas A&M Set to Take on Hurricanes Texas A&M takes to the field at home today to face the Miami Hurricanes in the ever played in College Station. All eyes will be on superstar running back Le’Veon Moss, who could play today after missing six straight games with an ankle injury. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

If not...

[Click HERE and I'll reveal what you're missing.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=f354f8036c9b489490e5249f18a79596&_e=jQ2c7at-fjcjWInHxY-lrQuVTx9Ac_MdEmmRI1tJKiarTiMdyef4Wb_p19g25xAr2hmtGq_YHAzN6Z3kSkfeKHBTe3KjcciLXyRDMw7HP1EuMwNF2iTRXC8sMcScdKudLMv7p0Bdx7o3DiwckbvgTsafRGx6iXgNtMu29ViZhL4GkkwwtssdptDXizirIQjFnTnDXHLmbY9-gQiTpURiKDFKH9B2hJJMyZyBsO3BNSiak4-xgxA8tELUY5_wQf_H4X3xFO8zNS3rgeIB1GBVPmZDtGHM5N0ZUQo9weID1P6c5DPTzdLuQ7F_NAf1-etdfzXDM4hWNC-tVuj-Il5boyaKTw0zD2dFLW40EXEcgVXQQNZdhrxHD1Zs_SrcoAK2S1xZMTnjkl13bClOExA1CK0tMyNSInQyqqzyddhNqFhZvvsuJDPfr0ZsxknDfavuZ2IV3pi-D2XQTOo3w3ORxMPMw6YZBg813-oMgBfgBEgEinMIIef9QpOa5hbZBa5-NGMN7I6Rg7hmMmqak9U91UyU34PrxiKHww5HenIl9ECxedoce8C-RAWZCiaEWsPr0Bafm82e4pYbWpn8sR9GjA%3D%3D)

You may be **shocked** by what you discover.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. name: Douglas Carswell
subject: From Magna Carta to Mass Arrests: Britain's Lesson for America
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/britainslessonforusa?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend,
**Imagine a world where you could face arrest simply for posting unkind or critical comments online.** Or picture police knocking on your door because you voiced opposition to mass immigration.

**You don’t need to imagine such a world – it is a reality in Britain, my former home and once part of the free world.**

Parents Maxie Allen and Rosalind Levine from Hertfordshire, England, were arrested earlier this year in front of their children over disparaging remarks made in a private parents' messaging group. They were among more than 10,000 people arrested in Britain this year for their online posts. **Britain last year arrested more people for what they wrote online than communist China.**
---
|
---
_“What’s all this, then?” - British police arresting parents_
| Of course, not all those arrested are sent to prison. But plenty are. **Lucy Connolly received a 31-month jail sentence after posting online, including a call for “mass deportation now.”**

Lucy Connolly and others have received “exemplary” sentences - in other words, instead of the British courts dispensing justice dispassionately, they have handed down arbitrary sentences designed to make an example of people, as one might expect in a third-world country.

For most people arrested in these cases, the process itself - months of uncertainty, reputational damage, family stress and the inability to earn a living - is intended to be the punishment. **Again, this is redolent of what you might find in a third world country, rather than the home of Magna Carta.**

**Is Britain today still a free country? Twenty or thirty years ago that would have been a ridiculous question. Today, it really isn’t. Free countries don’t behave this way. **

England, the country that gave the world common law no longer applies the law equally to all. Indeed, there have been cases of individuals receiving longer prison sentences for things they said online than some of those convicted in child grooming gang cases.

Only last week, Luke Yarwood was sent to jail for one and a half years for what he said online. That same week, Demiesh Williams, who literally beat a man to death in a supermarket queue, was sent to jail for three years. This is not what happens in a civilized Western society.

**Why should any of this matter to Americans? Because Britain is a bleak warning of what can happen to a formerly free society. **

Britain’s descent into anarcho-tyranny - where authorities are lax with serial offenders yet draconian toward those who strive to live law-abiding lives – has happened incredibly quickly.

To understand why the British state has turned against its own people, consider what occurred just last week on the other side of the world in Australia.

**During a Hanukkah celebration on Bondi Beach, two Pakistani men - one an immigrant to Australia, the other his son - massacred 16 people for being Jewish. **

Now reflect for a moment on how the Australian authorities responded.

Did the Aussie government announce that they’d be reviewing how such fanatics were allowed into the country? I didn’t hear it. Did the Canberra government question how it could be that a man born and raised in Australia might live in such a parallel culture inside Australia that he was willing to shoot children on a beach? No sign yet.

What I did hear is the Aussie Prime Minister insisting that diversity is Australia’s strength, followed by plans to further restrict gun ownership and curb free speech.

Rather than curb Muslim immigration to Australia, the lanyard wearing classes in Australia prefer to limit Australians' access to firearms.

The stated aim of the new free speech restrictions, apparently, is to prevent mobs chanting antisemitic messages - as occurred in the aftermath of Hamas’ October 7 atrocity near Sydney Opera House.

**You don’t need powers of prophecy to grasp that Australia’s new free speech restrictions will end up being used to lock up those that complain about Muslim migration more than they’ll ever be used to tackle extremist Islam. **

Instead of acknowledging and facing up to the problem of radical Islam, the authorities in Australia are trying to make it as much about tackling "hate" and "Islamophobia".

**Now do you begin to see why the authorities over in Britain have turned Soviet on their own citizens? **

The British public now sees the consequences of mass immigration from the third world – and they don’t like what they see. In the five years since I left Britain, something like 4 million immigrants from third-world countries have arrived gross.

Before Elon Musk bought X, the British authorities were able to rely on algorithms to try to suppress all sorts of dissent.

Now they can’t. Instead, they appear to be intentionally suppressing dissent through targeted prosecutions and prison sentences. The situation in Britain really is that grim now.

**How should America respond as Britain, and other Western states, descend into autocracy?** What might the United States do to try to ensure that what is happening in formerly free countries is just a spasm, and not a collapse?

1. **Cherish the First Amendment** : I know it's fashionable to claim that America has lost its way. Trust me, thanks to the Founders, the United States is looking far better than anywhere else on earth right now. **Value the Constitution, defend liberty ever more aggressively at home and never abandon the principle that in America you should be free to say things even if others find them offensive or foolish.**

1. **Stop treating allies as America’s equals if they mistreat their own citizens** : If Britain, Australia, and others start behaving like banana republics, treat their governments accordingly. Why should the United States provide defense subsidies and a diplomatic premium to a regime that fall so far short of Western standards of behavior?

1. **End mass immigration** : Control your country’s borders, or your government will end up trying to control you to prevent complaints about the consequences of uncontrolled borders.

1. **Support dissidents** : During the Cold War, Ronald Reagan made it clear that the United States stood with Soviet dissidents like Natan Sharansky. As Sharansky explained in his brilliant book, that moral support was crucial in the fight against tyranny. **The US State Department should proactively identify and assist dissidents in Britain and elsewhere, in some cases offering asylum to those persecuted by their own governments.**

A year or so ago, I facetiously suggested that if things got really bad in Britain, Donald Trump could apply the ultimate pressure by offering every Brit under the age of 30 the right to work in America. If he did so, there would be a mass exodus and the government in London would collapse. That idea no longer seems quite so absurd.
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=c5499703d6&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
|
---
| | _ _
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013
[Add us to your address book](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/vcard?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=26cb690c9a) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=26cb690c9a).

_Copyright © 2025 Mississippi Center for Public Policy, All rights reserved._

It’s not that I don’t want to (go outside) — I simply can’t.” Then he said something even darker: “Life is on hardcore mode. You make one mistake, you’re dead. It only takes one mistake.” Let that sink in, Peter. Elon is SCARED FOR HIS LIFE because he: It’s clear the Swamp wants Elon Musk SILENCED — PERMANENTLY. He can’t even go outside with the threat of being killed! That’s why we’re asking 25,000 PATRIOTS: The attacks will only get worse. We MUST show Elon that America is behind him!
