clean_body: Marine, Pastor, Humanitarian, Patriot A state built on faith, family, freedom, and the Second Amendment is being tested -- and its future will shape the country. | | | | | Peter, | | If this is the first time you're hearing from me, let me introduce myself. My name is Victor Marx -- a husband, father, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and founder of an international humanitarian organization that has carried out more than 150 high-risk rescue missions around the world.I've spent my life stepping into the chaos most people run from, fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. I never expected to run for Governor of Colorado... But people I respected deeply -- including Charlie and Erika Kirk -- believed in me and urged me to step forward because they saw what was happening here in our state.After Charlie was killed, Erika reminded me that he believed this was a fight I was meant to take on.They were right, and I am now fully committed to winning this fight. Because here's the truth most people outside our state don't realize: Colorado SHOULD still be a red state. Colorado was built on independence, limited government, faith, family, and the unshakeable belief in the Second Amendment. Those values run deep in our history and in our people.But over time, the political establishment and a flood of left-wing ideas have overpowered the very values that made Colorado strong. We've watched our state shift from red, to purple, to blue -- not because the people changed, but because leadership failed them.And that failure matters far beyond our borders. Colorado has become a testing ground for policies that spread nationwide -- soft-on-crime agendas, government overreach, attacks on faith, restrictions on constitutional freedoms. If we stop that tide here, we stop it from sweeping across America. That's why this race is so important. If Colorado flips back, the entire country will feel it. It will prove that conservative, constitutional values aren't fading -- they 're rising again! Our state and country has too many politicians , so instead, I'm asking you to stand with ME - a Marine, pastor, humanitarian, and Patriot - to ensure we have leaders who will fight to uphold and protect our values and freedoms. This is our chance to send that message. This is our chance to restore a state that should never have been lost.And this is the moment every patriot -- in Colorado and across America -- should care about. If you're just learning about me today, I hope it's the beginning of something bigger. Because this movement isn't powered by mega-donors or coastal elites -- Thank you for standing with me! For Colorado. For America. Victor Marx Conservative for Governor of Colorado | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | PAID FOR BY VICTOR MARX FOR GOVERNOR Registered Agent: Gregory Carlson | | P.O. Box 156 Florence, CO 81226

subject: Democratic Trifecta in Wisconsin
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity
| Hi, We’ve reached out a lot these past few weeks, and there’s a reason for that: there’s a lot at stake in this race. I know you have heard that before, but we have been working towards this moment for over a decade in Wisconsin. **This election is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to not only hold this Governor’s seat, but to build a Democratic trifecta in the state of Wisconsin for the first time in over a decade.** At a time when the Trump administration is trampling on our pocketbooks, healthcare, and democracy, a Democratic trifecta would allow us to actually defend our state and legislate for a better future. **[Can we count on your support to help elect Mandela and build a Democratic trifecta at this critical point for Wisconsin?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122025_B1_Stakes&t=2&akid=5727%2E196590%2Er2gZkV)** _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now._ | | | [DONATE $15 »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/6574?amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122025_B1_Stakes&t=3&akid=5727%2E196590%2Er2gZkV)

It’s not going to be easy. But it wasn’t easy for Tony Evers and Mandela Barnes to oust Scott Walker in 2018 or for Wisconsin to finally unrig our maps that were some of the most gerrymandered in the country. Nothing worth fighting for is easy, but I know that together we can get this done.
With new legislative maps, we have a real opportunity to secure a trifecta that allows our government to actually govern for everyday Wisconsinites, to achieve real progress.
The enthusiasm for this race has been astounding, and it’s crucial that we keep up the energy. This election is going to be competitive, and tens of millions of dollars are going to be spent by out-of-state Republican special interest groups.
**[If you’re ready for the chance to make generational change here in Wisconsin, will you contribute $15 or $27 to our campaign today?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122025_B1_Stakes&t=9&akid=5727%2E196590%2Er2gZkV)**

I know you have heard that before, but we have been working towards this moment for over a decade in Wisconsin. This election is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to not only hold this Governor’s seat, but to build a Democratic trifecta in the state of Wisconsin for the first time in over a decade. At a time when the Trump administration is trampling on our pocketbooks, healthcare, and democracy, a Democratic trifecta would allow us to actually defend our state and legislate for a better future. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | | | | | | | | | | It’s not going to be easy. But it wasn’t easy for Tony Evers and Mandela Barnes to oust Scott Walker in 2018 or for Wisconsin to finally unrig our maps that were some of the most gerrymandered in the country. Nothing worth fighting for is easy, but I know that together we can get this done. With new legislative maps, we have a real opportunity to secure a trifecta that allows our government to actually govern for everyday Wisconsinites, to achieve real progress. The enthusiasm for this race has been astounding, and it’s crucial that we keep up the energy. This election is going to be competitive, and tens of millions of dollars are going to be spent by out-of-state Republican special interest groups. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | | | | | | | | | Thank you, Team Mandela | | Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin P.O. Box 90541 Milwaukee, WI 53209

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: MGP's gift of Coal. How fitting at Christmas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITSxjytRqnk5Se65PvvR0SC1Vi60eIcmGaf4kR6MPJnbRGd2XwIrhDn8ZZ-Z4PPuO0Vx2PdmM8zh_l0Ht__s0eBODdMFm5jpBV5pYsNqlpM6-yn_qJNmCbrn8CU9k-N0OJ2Ey54OBrPwPF57P7greV4HmT9jb6vD7EWPY4HK7hE0Hkc9DEhp6B1Cndsj8SVELf/4ml/8HQHE8K-Snmpy9DBU6PA0g/h0/h001.CxqoOOLNQ5KzkHThEgtRCY48bnfULVpSVmSHYJQ0HRM)
---
Peter,

Congress ended its session and went home for the holidays without healthcare subsidies in hand. Instead, it literally handed us **coal burning** for electricity as a year end bow. Let's recap what MGP was able to secure for the district: She voted for the Kayla Hamilton Act that allows for unaccompanied immigrant **children to be strip-searched.** She voted for the SPEED Act, which will **weaken** the National Environmental Protection Agency. She voted for the Mining Regulatory Clarity Act, which will make **mining** on **public lands easier**. She voted for the Do No Harm in Medicaid Act, which **prohibits** Medicaid funds from being used to provide **gender-affirming care. **
---
[ **Hold MGP Accoutable Now** ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqssGssCLo7XSTKmfgwTIHYuEAgiC5gHrXc5Kv1Yh2sj60tM99hnZ9l_X1RqonLHAYaJQ2QU-riBu9tojcbYCp3zYqF3yjMuSnzr4w9efs4OAUwonJ6TasGoNCadu_OZhlJthCriImILFH_KbQxSNZ2uCUOxfDiSWMSJB-FRK3-WmVlKVj90w83_NW_49Kuvi0aSc8m_lDeilwwlGdlJaH_lhOU0G7UkaC1ZZyuYJhS6r92H2wCqKLHOeX5-3q7mlw7Nxm1Pe6wtK_fo9zlYxI5g/4ml/8HQHE8K-Snmpy9DBU6PA0g/h1/h001.hDE1OqEjWQxRflRVvuXciDU2_lunXltlUrUQ8Wel96Q)
---
What Congresswoman Marie Gluesenkamp Perez delivered this week was a slap in the face. She says she always votes her conscience and what's best for her constituents. I have the same principle, but I apply it much differently than she does. I would have **never** voted for these **ridiculous bills** that further weaken environmental protections, due process rights, and the safety of trans people. I would have been **fighting to secure healthcare subsidies** before the end of the year. Instead, MGP decided the Republicans' pinky promise on subsidies was enough and ended up walking away with nothing to show for it. [**Can you rush $10, $25, or $50 help replace Marie with someone who will actually stand for Democratic values?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqssGssCLo7XSTKmfgwTIHYuEAgiC5gHrXc5Kv1Yh2sj6xiDrmk7tNhjGUbTsPW5fu_WH_9zJ_9hJs2X43Ib3aBct1tbQNiqfBFDhXZVVczWaJDU3hMikWxozzZHgwnhwVcROQziFFHtAx9QeJ3f1vYvHnsFHNiRQGMxXP-0K12WScTJ9OgcM5hy5HiWh8FG-dPlbP5gcaMp4UDU5QGDfHlT4NUgkbNGE-6YIGN0gMIFM4n_xOhuDKl40aJ3ZXeV27tgQb8xIlVb7iU8ibTf2FgE/4ml/8HQHE8K-Snmpy9DBU6PA0g/h2/h001.UZpoCqp0IimnmkriaAJCy2BK5RdwX30GI83onK2ZL1c) We need to show strong support to send a message that this will not stand in this district. We must VOTE BLUE, BUT IT MATTERS WHO!
Let's Heal Washington!
Happy Holidays from the entire team at Brent Hennrich for Washington. Brent Hennrich

clean_body: | | Peter, Congress ended its session and went home for the holidays without healthcare subsidies in hand. Instead, it literally handed us coal burning for electricity as a year end bow. Let's recap what MGP was able to secure for the district: She voted for the Kayla Hamilton Act that allows for unaccompanied immigrant children to be strip-searched. She voted for the SPEED Act, which will weaken the National Environmental Protection Agency. She voted for the Mining Regulatory Clarity Act, which will make mining on public lands easier. She voted for the Do No Harm in Medicaid Act, which prohibits Medicaid funds from being used to provide gender-affirming care. What Congresswoman Marie Gluesenkamp Perez delivered this week was a slap in the face. She says she always votes her conscience and what's best for her constituents. I have the same principle, but I apply it much differently than she does. I would have never voted for these ridiculous bills that further weaken environmental protections, due process rights, and the safety of trans people. I would have been fighting to secure healthcare subsidies before the end of the year. Instead, MGP decided the Republicans' pinky promise on subsidies was enough and ended up walking away with nothing to show for it. We need to show strong support to send a message that this will not stand in this district. We must VOTE BLUE, BUT IT MATTERS WHO! Let's Heal Washington! Happy Holidays from the entire team at Brent Hennrich for Washington. Brent Hennrich | | Marie Gluesenkamp-Perez has betrayed Democrats and the voters of WA-3 by repeatedly voting with MAGA against women's rights, immigrants, enabling Trump, and more. Brent Hennrich is a proud Washingtonian, a working class dad, and lifelong Democratic activist. body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121687521/590250801/1545041525?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.20_Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YmM1OTIxYy1lM2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvOUzJmUO6tLMcEE1uvMpNH2vCLnFYnD0U47iBQ9ii8=) Hey folks, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. clean_body: We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. = Hey folks, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mark Kelly and Kirsten Gillibrand will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = Thank you. name: USO Newsletter Team
subject: Happy Holidays! We're excited to share our latest newsletter with you
body:
Stories of your generosity supporting our service members worldwide.
| | | |
---
| | | [](https://links.e.uso.)
---
|
---
| [GIVE NOW »](https://links.e.uso.)
---

| |

| | YOUR USO AT WORK
---
|
---
_Issue 137 / December 2025_

| | |
---

[](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/zdb4h379v_Jp/RWxqYWZ3Z1UxNmFZZ3NUUWp5SnFydStmWVdjNHdGL2Y0bm1TQis2cUtySVhPZ3NGQUIrM1dHWG93MndIZnNiNVpXRURLL3djcWtyalk2ZHpVNG42ZUtHblhzVUlxSkUxd1BseUhaSmQyUGM9S0/NkNhalErOE1sQnBHYTFWaGQrNUpwdGhPSWxFeVRJS1NpdldZNWZSRUdmTFV4cVhFdHprVDhVV1dZUGgwbkdBZERSN21BSE9pSHJXODJDYlk0UnM2OUdVYVNnbDhIR0EyS0)

USO Supporter,

The holiday season is upon us, and it's more important than ever to remember those serving far from home and their loved ones. Thanks to the generosity of our supporters, the USO is there to deliver care packages, provide calls home, serve holiday meals and create meaningful memories for our heroes, no matter where they serve.

| | | USO FRONT LINES
---|---

[](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/E89~Cy7X4~hZ/RWxqYWZ3Z1UxNmFZZ3NUUWp5SnFydStmWVdjNHdGL2Y0bm1TQis2cUtySVhPZ3NGQUIrM1dHWG93MndIZnNiNVpXRURLL3djcWtyalk2ZHpVNG42ZUtHblhzVUlxSkUxd1BseUhaSmQyUGM9S0/NkNhalErOE1sQnBHYTFWaGQrNUpwdGhPSWxFeVRJS1NpdldZNWZSRUdmTFV4cVhFdHprVDhVV1dZUGgwbkdBZERSN21BSE9pSHJXODJDYlk0UnM2OUdVYVNnbDhIR0EyS0)

**USO Cookie Week celebrates military communities worldwide**
From San Antonio to South Korea, USO Cookie Week gave service members and their families the chance to gather, bake, decorate and enjoy sweet treats all around the world. It was a simple reminder of the joy of connections and the little moments that make the holidays special. [**Learn more.**](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/dp4vCPa_gRJR/RWxqYWZ3Z1UxNmFZZ3NUUWp5SnFydStmWVdjNHdGL2Y0bm1TQis2cUtySVhPZ3NGQUIrM1dHWG93MndIZnNiNVpXRURLL3djcWtyalk2ZHpVNG42ZUtHblhzVUlxSkUxd1BseUhaSmQyUGM9S0/NkNhalErOE1sQnBHYTFWaGQrNUpwdGhPSWxFeVRJS1NpdldZNWZSRUdmTFV4cVhFdHprVDhVV1dZUGgwbkdBZERSN21BSE9pSHJXODJDYlk0UnM2OUdVYVNnbDhIR0EyS0)

| | | GLOBAL ENTERTAINMENT
---|---

Pictured: Jeff Ross (front right) and ERNEST (front left)

**USO Holiday Tour Recap**
In just seven days, the 2025 USO Holiday Tour traveled nearly 20,000 miles across five countries. Entertainers including NFL legend Vince Wilfork, comedians Jeff Ross and Mia Jackson and country artists ERNEST and Cody Lohden brought laughter and music to service members spending the holidays far from home.

**Craig Robinson helps spread holiday cheer**
Last month, Craig Robinson joined Chase DuRousseau and Jimmy Shin to bring laughs and music to troops stationed across the Republic of Korea. In addition to performing for packed crowds, the entertainers spent time learning about missions, meeting K-9 teams and connecting with service members during meet-and-greet events that created lasting memories.

**Rob Riggle visits service members at Camp Pendleton and Fort Riley**
The USO recently welcomed actor, comedian and retired Marine Corps Lieutenant Colonel Rob Riggle for a two-stop tour at Camp Pendleton and Fort Riley. Across both installations, Riggle connected with more than 825 service members and families, sharing candid stories from his 23 years of service and delivering messages of resilience and humor shaped by his time in uniform and in Hollywood.

| | |
---
| | | CORPORATE PARTNERS
---|---

**Jeep ® teams up with the USO to send holiday care packages **

More than 300 Jeep® employees assembled nearly 10,000 USO Holiday Care Packages for service members deployed overseas. Filled with essentials and comforts from home, these packages will reach troops in more than 70 countries, reminding them they aren't forgotten this holiday season.

**Partner Spotlight: American Airlines**

As a USO President's Circle partner, American Airlines is honored to support America's service members and their families through USO programs. AAdvantage®️ members can donate miles to the USO via Miles for Heroes. Every mile, every donation helps the USO support our nation's heroes. **[Learn more.](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/WYqRCr73bLT_/RWxqYWZ3Z1UxNmFZZ3NUUWp5SnFydStmWVdjNHdGL2Y0bm1TQis2cUtySVhPZ3NGQUIrM1dHWG93MndIZnNiNVpXRURLL3djcWtyalk2ZHpVNG42ZUtHblhzVUlxSkUxd1BseUhaSmQyUGM9S0/)**

| | | [](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/ZdvWh04~9WFR/RWxqYWZ3Z1UxNmFZZ3NUUWp5SnFydStmWVdjNHdGL2Y0bm1TQis2cUtySVhPZ3NGQUIrM1dHWG93MndIZnNiNVpXRURLL3djcWtyalk2ZHpVNG42ZUtHblhzVUlxSkUxd1BseUhaSmQyUGM9S0/NkNhalErOE1sQnBHYTFWaGQrNUpwdGhPSWxFeVRJS1NpdldZNWZSRUdmTFV4cVhFdHprVDhVV1dZUGgwbkdBZERSN21BSE9pSHJXODJDYlk0UnM2OUdVYVNnbDhIR0EyS0)
---

The USO is there for our troops and their families through every step of their military journey, from the moment they enlist, through every deployment and as they transition back to civilian life. Giving monthly to the USO means making the biggest impact for America's service members and military families.

[**Set up your monthly gift now. >>**](https://links.e.uso.)

| |
---
Thank you for supporting
our nation's heroes.

name: Kellyanne Conway
subject: America desperately needs to hear this message
body:
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=435441e87c2c44cdace5d985b7fff431&_e=5QFItp7zmZ1mKi9iqiJg1qoH_T6flwyd1ah1hyR5efn98m_5LsUvv_MoXCcyNKaiIMTnVjuhScXkG8CbH-_dse7vUJS1qyq8kQW_L7YR6CRvXDH4X4mz0JdGr7vqnayjq-K2hJMGQ7Chf7R9d8P7daDL5L3DnxcWZ6DqcRCCoDtfn8WmCApdTidjpxXVxkRfIYuQTaIjT2c350lAj5oOY1w_KspuA_Th9mHGSShdhCcuH17FQIhqV8z8024ahlzoxJgU2hOuKodXWWtNbs4eNuGhMrTb07kph2M7cPfrmCMYXV6KfblndnHL5IB7xeSvn4A21mTnS72Nt2bYjLK1xzsZ2PNHkJ80iYllp6Dg22nnRyBMNKUzGizWdJhKIE9F86zbRemcSEXr7WxhFgYoJfeOxPfGuZKOVMZmla20xg8EKbnmdrcBxpAGNr3OG2zO)
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=435441e87c2c44cdace5d985b7fff431&_e=5QFItp7zmZ1mKi9iqiJg1qoH_T6flwyd1ah1hyR5efn98m_5LsUvv_MoXCcyNKaiIMTnVjuhScXkG8CbH-_dse7vUJS1qyq8kQW_L7YR6CRvXDH4X4mz0JdGr7vqnayjq-K2hJMGQ7Chf7R9d8P7daDL5L3DnxcWZ6DqcRCCoDsl5wIwNfybXJplPnPvACeTbBaCy-8wI5x5KiCH2Vnqya8iUxKzFTFsmUupho_dGFhFCqjr6DGpo66PCM6URcqeoRcS83tZQ0wW6SRDW0Jmz3_k37OyGr0h8Djr98KULRd_BN-bcDOrLJUfhrtkUbfoyLx8vRoUNJ0pnn1fovfTkEO2LDJi0aT4_e7lQluqXWI5yM233WiwPHCUxlj3S8tGh9RDPx3jSFXF1NG_8L8weuhwDJ5PLb_kY11nk8W2eoSWnXuuQrWDTivAZq24oYQZARsCtCiGoq3Up_Y8fVjnPA%3D%3D)

This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before.

No, this isn't about a campaign or an election.

I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society.

Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

name: Team Wes
subject: “Wes Moore Is The Education Governor”
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1095%2E1086043%2Efr3rbZ)
**“Wes Moore is the Education Governor.”**
Peter, that’s what a Baltimore City teacher wrote into the Baltimore Sun this month, highlighting the wins Wes has put on the board in our fight to make sure our kids have the best public education possible.
The Moore-Miller administration is reducing the teacher vacancy rate, getting record funding for Maryland’s public schools three years in a row, and investing billions in our public school system. We’re strengthening pathways for federal workers to grow into education careers, and uplifting our young men and boys by prioritizing recruitment and retention for male educators.
And it’s getting results: Recent reporting shows that nearly _every single grade_ in Maryland has made improvements in reading and math scores.
**[That’s what it means when we leave no one behind. If you’re on board with this vision for Maryland, will you chip in any amount to help reelect Wes Moore today?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251220-2.cRWSyB&refcode=em-20251220-2&t=2&akid=1095%2E1086043%2Efr3rbZ)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251220-2.cRWSyB&amount=5&refcode=em-20251220-2&t=21&akid=1095%2E1086043%2Efr3rbZ)**
Thanks, Peter.
-- Team Wes

BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER
clean_body: “Wes Moore is the Education Governor.” Peter, that’s what a Baltimore City teacher wrote into the Baltimore Sun this month, highlighting the wins Wes has put on the board in our fight to make sure our kids have the best public education possible. The Moore-Miller administration is reducing the teacher vacancy rate, getting record funding for Maryland’s public schools three years in a row, and investing billions in our public school system. We’re strengthening pathways for federal workers to grow into education careers, and uplifting our young men and boys by prioritizing recruitment and retention for male educators. And it’s getting results: Recent reporting shows that nearly every single grade in Maryland has made improvements in reading and math scores. Thanks, Peter. -- Team Wes

# _Opinion:_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8a5748f3-b40d-4c97-bacb-76b0ea1b662c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

### Become a PolitiBrawl member to support bold independent journalism!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..rS1p8MdouZadjgCV8M_qPhksr49w2qz3NVl1s-yinxM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182194304)
* * *
**After Vice President Kamala Harris was beaten** by then-former President Donald Trump in the 2024 election, the Democratic National Committee was despondent. How could "brat" Harris not win? She was endorsed by the likes of Beyonce and Robert Reich! She was a black, Indian woman who raised over one billion dollars (and somehow still ended up in debt)!
Well, the DNC set about conducting an internal autopsy report to figure out what went wrong in 2024. While anyone with a non-fried brain can see the many reasons why Democrats were swept away that year, Democrats wanted to set the narrative on why they lost so badly.
At least, that was the plan until recently. The New York Times **[reported](https://substack.com/redirect/24cb1a09-59c4-4e83-bd3c-78b0fee3d20c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** DNC Chairman Ken Martin announced the party will not publish the report and keep it sealed.
"Here's our North Star: Does this help us win?" Martin said in a statement. "If the answer is no, it's a distraction from the core mission."
_**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**_
* * *
**[Together with Health Sciences Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[RFK Jr just NUKED liberals #1 source of campaign funds](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
RFK Jr just launched a _**NUKE**_ on **[liberals #1 source of dark money campaign contributions](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** ...
And **THEY ARE** _**TERRIFIED**_.
Friend, if RFK Jr pulls **[THIS](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** off, it could set back the liberal agenda by _**DECADES**_.
But more importantly, it could also save your life.
**[Click here now](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to see what has liberal elites _**QUAKING**_ in their penny loafers.
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. Liberals and their big money donors are going to fight tooth and nail to stop you from hearing about this. So we can't be sure how much longer this video will be available online. **[Please watch this now](https://substack.com/redirect/2e0417eb-1113-4c64-8c31-d9b4811900a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and spread the word while the video is still up.
* * *
_**(Piece continues)**_
The lack of will to publicly admit the reasons for their loss has been strengthened by Democrats winning many special election races this year, providing the much-needed boost to claim they are well on their way to winning the midterms next year.
Even though some Democratic operatives have praised the move of not rehashing the 2024 election once again, others have said this only proves the "old guard" are not learning anything meaningful.
"Arguing that releasing an autopsy would be too divisive and show that we're in disarray is such bullsh*t. Voters want to see how the party is actually learning from its mistakes and fighting to figure out the pathway forward rather than saying now is not the time as we always do," gun control activist and former DNC vice chair David Hogg said on X.
[| | | | | David Hogg 🟧
---
@davidhogg111
Arguing that releasing an autopsy would be too divisive and show that we're in disarray is such bullshit. Voters want to see how the party is actually learning from its mistakes and fighting to figure out the pathway forward rather than saying now is not the time as we always do
| 9:15 PM * Dec 18, 2025 * 96.6K Views97 Replies * 208 Reposts * 1.47K Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/14473c7d-828c-4c3d-b858-89e2285d9e0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The lack of public pressure to enact change due to not releasing the report does project the image that Democrats will just continue doubling down on their disastrous policies.
Just this week, **[a Quinnipiac University National Poll](https://substack.com/redirect/cff56bd7-d756-4abf-8c01-4e0f9ae013dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** found only 18 percent of voters approve of the way the Democrats in Congress are handling their job. That dissatisfaction does not necessarily mean their voters won't turn out for them in 2026, but it shows the Democratic Party is facing strong headwinds despite the constant attacks against Trump and the GOP.
The funniest example of this is Rep. Jasmine Crockett jumping into the Senate primary in Texas. It has been reported the GOP pushed polling that had the obnoxious congresswoman in the lead among the field, which contributed to Crockett declaring her candidacy. It is true she does well with the progressive base, but her past actions and insane comments while in Congress will be tough to defend to the general population in the Lone Star State.
It is still early to say for sure if the GOP can defy historic trends about the midterms or if Democrats are sure to sweep the House, but this recent decision by the DNC could come back to haunt them if the Blue Wave fails to materialize.

clean_body: | = Hi Peter, Cherlynn Stevenson here. The Cook Political Report just named our race in Kentucky's 6th Congressional District as one of the top opportunities for a Democratic pick-up in the House next year. That means Republicans will be spending big to stop our movement — I need your help to fight back. **=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = All I want is for the place that I love to have what it needs to thrive. And when the day comes where Congress finally finds itself back on track, let it be said that Kentucky's 6th Congressional District led the way. Thank you, Cherlynn (personal) Candidate for KY-06 Former Kentucky Minority Caucus Chair = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. #
---
Here’s something that sounds too good to be true, Peter: **If everyone reading this email pitched in just $1, we’d raise more in an _hour_ than we do in a week.** It’s true! $1 may not seem like a lot — especially when Republican campaigns are being bankrolled by billionaires — the same billionaires who saw a return on their investment when Republicans cut their taxes by trillions of dollars, paid for at the expense of our neediest families. But when hundreds of people chip in $1 or more at a time, it adds up to something big fast. We understand that between buying gifts for loved ones and holiday travel, money can get pretty tight around this time of the year — and we would never ask you to give more than you can. But a small $1 donation today would go a long way toward supporting Mazie’s fight for families in the Senate and her work to win the Senate majority for Democrats next year. **[So if you can, Peter — and _only_ if you can — can you chip in just $1? clean_body: | Here’s something that sounds too good to be true, Peter: If everyone reading this email pitched in just $1, we’d raise more in an hour than we do in a week. It’s true! $1 may not seem like a lot — especially when Republican campaigns are being bankrolled by billionaires — the same billionaires who saw a return on their investment when Republicans cut their taxes by trillions of dollars, paid for at the expense of our neediest families. But when hundreds of people chip in $1 or more at a time, it adds up to something big fast. We understand that between buying gifts for loved ones and holiday travel, money can get pretty tight around this time of the year — and we would never ask you to give more than you can. But a small $1 donation today would go a long way toward supporting Mazie’s fight for families in the Senate and her work to win the Senate majority for Democrats next year. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: ** | | = = | = = | = = Regardless of whether you can give, your support makes a huge difference. name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: What I said on MSNOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
Let’s prove that we’re going to show up everywhere |
#
---
Team, I recently joined Jen Psaki on MSNOW to talk about our work. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121688643/590254000/-1313101069?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_FLHP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20251220_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjBjMWRlYzgtZThkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=HNAxMYZjOutFmHlhINi8UnvKtS3IKcd2qK8kziSz0aM=) On her show, I shared a story about our Fight Like Hell PAC training in Wisconsin. I was meeting with voters at a local bar, and a man named Bill working in the kitchen came around from behind the counter and told us, _“No one’s talking to the middle class anymore. My health care costs keep going up.**We need help.** ”_ That conversation stuck with me — because he’s right. Too many people feel like no one is showing up for them. That’s exactly why I started Fight Like Hell PAC, and it’s guiding our strategy as we prepare for upcoming elections. **I’m working hard to recruit and train candidates and staff who listen, meet people where they are, and focus on what really matters** — lowering costs, putting money back in people’s pockets, and making sure everyone has what they need to get ahead. **[I’m asking you to help us show up. We can only do this with grassroots support. Pitch in right now, and let’s get to work:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121688645/590254002/-1313101069?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_FLHP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20251220_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjBjMWRlYzgtZThkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=HNAxMYZjOutFmHlhINi8UnvKtS3IKcd2qK8kziSz0aM=)** | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121688647/590254004/-1313101069?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_FLHP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20251220_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjBjMWRlYzgtZThkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=HNAxMYZjOutFmHlhINi8UnvKtS3IKcd2qK8kziSz0aM=)
---
We’ve seen this strategy work in Michigan. When we show up in every community, we can feed more kids at school, cut retirement taxes, and make sure folks who work overtime actually see that pay reflected in their paycheck. That’s what we just did with our bipartisan budget package.
And this is the vision I’m working to scale across the country, **but National Republicans are already flooding the airwaves and organizing in places they think we won’t go.** If we want to keep winning, we have to meet people on their turf — and that takes resources, people, and grit.
So here’s my question:
**How are people going to know what we're fighting for if we don’t show up?[Let’s prove that we will. Chip in before our 2025 fundraising deadline to help keep Fight Like Hell PAC showing up — everywhere. Just like we’ve done in Michigan, Wisconsin, Florida, and New Jersey.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121688648/590254005/-1313101069?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_FLHP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20251220_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjBjMWRlYzgtZThkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=HNAxMYZjOutFmHlhINi8UnvKtS3IKcd2qK8kziSz0aM=)**

clean_body: Let’s prove that we’re going to show up everywhere | Team, I recently joined Jen Psaki on MSNOW to talk about our work. = On her show, I shared a story about our Fight Like Hell PAC training in Wisconsin. I was meeting with voters at a local bar, and a man named Bill working in the kitchen came around from behind the counter and told us, “No one’s talking to the middle class anymore. My health care costs keep going up.We need help. ” That conversation stuck with me — because he’s right. Too many people feel like no one is showing up for them. That’s exactly why I started Fight Like Hell PAC, and it’s guiding our strategy as we prepare for upcoming elections. I’m working hard to recruit and train candidates and staff who listen, meet people where they are, and focus on what really matters — lowering costs, putting money back in people’s pockets, and making sure everyone has what they need to get ahead. = | | = We’ve seen this strategy work in Michigan. When we show up in every community, we can feed more kids at school, cut retirement taxes, and make sure folks who work overtime actually see that pay reflected in their paycheck. That’s what we just did with our bipartisan budget package. And this is the vision I’m working to scale across the country, but National Republicans are already flooding the airwaves and organizing in places they think we won’t go. If we want to keep winning, we have to meet people on their turf — and that takes resources, people, and grit. So here’s my question: How are people going to know what we're fighting for if we don’t show up?= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = | = = | = = Thanks for being part of this fight. Onward, Gretchen Fight Like Hell PAC PO Box 15093 Lansing, MI 48901 United States ** | mailto:info@fightlikehellpac.org| | Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC. FightLikeHellPAC.org. ICE, a disabled veteran, a habeas petition, and whether constitutional loyalty is rewarded--or punished.

# [Liberty and Justice for All, Without an Asterisk](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=182152493&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTUyNDkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYyNjYyMzAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODg1ODIzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5A660f9jFth5O_j9TQscmX2AL_FldRl73FHSiKJ5iMc)
### ICE, a disabled veteran, a habeas petition, and whether constitutional loyalty is rewarded--or punished.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Dec 20
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=182152493&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTUyNDkzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NjI2NjIzMCwiZXhwIjoxNzY4ODU4MjMwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.HZ-qO6ym4_krYwFRtu35x39Pbv3R8ZhXMaitBgLSnv8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=182152493&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTUyNDkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYyNjYyMzAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODg1ODIzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5A660f9jFth5O_j9TQscmX2AL_FldRl73FHSiKJ5iMc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=182152493&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTUyNDkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYyNjYyMzAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODg1ODIzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5A660f9jFth5O_j9TQscmX2AL_FldRl73FHSiKJ5iMc)
---
| | [](https://substack..z84l96HiOdkTeZthI_OSO-VjY0LNFOu_vciHKsvhdrY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/liberty-and-justice-for-all-without?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

On Monday morning, my husband, **Muhammad Zahid Chaudhry** , will appear before a federal judge in the Western District of Washington for a habeas hearing. The Court could order his immediate release - or even, after a quarter-century of eligibility, his immediate naturalization.
We are approaching this with seriousness, restraint, and deep respect for the Court's process. We are not here to litigate in public, and we are not here to inflame. We are here because for the first time in 25 years, the _full record_ of my husband's case is before an Article III judge with Constitutional authority.
That matters.
Zahid is an honorably discharged, disabled United States Army veteran. He has lived in this country lawfully for more than two decades. He served his community, served his country, and swore an oath to protect and defend the Constitution of the United States.
**Early in his military service, immediately post-9/11, he was pressured by intelligence agencies to participate in unlawful targeting and surveillance of innocent Americans.**
**He refused** --not because he lacked loyalty, but because what was being asked of him was wrong. He would have shared information about any genuine threat without hesitation. What he would not do was violate the Constitution.
**That principled, lawful, Constitutional refusal is the through-line of the last quarter century.**
For 25 years, Zahid's _earned_ citizenship has been delayed, denied, and used as blackmail to compel his compliance. He's been harassed, targeted, defamed, smeared, and now detained. **Not because he failed America --but because he honored it.** That is not rhetoric. That is what the record shows when it is allowed to be seen whole.
* * *
The habeas hearing on Monday is not about sympathy. It is about _law_. It is about whether the government can continue to unlawfully detain a disabled veteran, grabbed in an illegal hallway abduction, claim legality under a legally meaningless removal order, deny him adequate medical care, and then change its story when judicial review becomes unavoidable.
But at root, it is about **whether constitutional loyalty is rewarded --or punished**--in this country.
We are not asking the public to pre-judge the outcome. We are asking for something simpler and more fundamental: **that the rule of law be allowed to function.**
* * *
If you are coming to the courthouse, please do so calmly and respectfully. Quiet presence matters. Dignity matters. The Court must be allowed to do its work without pressure or spectacle.
Whatever the immediate outcome, something irreversible has already happened: the truth is now on the record. It cannot be buried again.
* * *
Last year, I ran for Congress on the simple, essential premise that **" Liberty and Justice for All" doesn't come with an asterisk.** I spoke to countless people, from all countries and all walks of life, the message that "If someone comes here believing in the founding principles of America and wants to contribute to this country, they are already American, no matter what the paperwork says."
_**Especially**_ if that someone swore an oath to defend the Constitution; broke his body in service to that oath; and sacrificed _every_ kind of stability, comfort, and reward to uphold that oath -- when it would have been easier, safer, and more personally profitable to obey corrupt authority and betray.
I am the granddaughter of a Nazi concentration camp survivor, the daughter of a U.S. Navy veteran, and the wife of a disabled Army veteran. I am the mother of two small children whose names mean **Peace** and **Justice**. I believe in this country in my blood and bones--not as an abstraction, but as a set of principles that govern us, and a woven fabric of obligations we owe to one another.
Monday is about whether those obligations still mean something. Whether loyalty to the Constitution will be rewarded... or punished.
I will share an update after the hearing.
Thank you, all, for your steadfast and unwavering support. America needs us, now.
--
Melissa

Marjorie Taylor Greene shocked the nation when she announced she'll resign from Congress on January 5, 2026. That resignation has triggered a special election in GA‐14. **And this district -- our district -- is suddenly on the brink of deciding more than just one seat. ****_It could determine who controls Congress. _**
I'm Shawn Harris. I'm a Democrat. Husband. Father. Retired Brigadier General. name: Rep. Brianna Titone
subject: Hanukkah blessings to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brianna for Treasurer
body:
As we gather with loved ones and prepare for tomorrow’s celebrations, I want to wish you a Merry Christmas! | | | [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/87mDLU3nyPqsApZTVctyu5cweHZvpf-EruUdUKgBmwdAEfNQdhU3yu6IR_kdbh1luhH-nMT4CPX82n4A-1SbHlRKJSdkhnM_h5iSwIEiLo-0BUcapf6RFMS0HrUmyZuZcSOxBVuy4I_ocoAia14GNovfFgVmo7a0OgB1E6QPSrSpRY_dpzJ8L-smRaXqnngoaTMT7YMmdvbhPoeVsZpPL3pg2g-jB9KxeJ7CY022yUgm3-E5VmKH_7sesVuSvNfT_XV0io5WI0pYOPoo3s0aHV4ucISQPlMmALq-xfzSl66NJ1vfBHH0g4BuMmb_CkD7biI7rxHv36fU2ccvwc0hR-BdPOg4OwgtXZsonstw-u1mTNhomCQXJPNpRvcyQYl28VN-kek/o1fZHWmn69IrXrkpJBXs5lAJVVu2Mnwh/16) Hi Peter, Thursday’s Hanukkiah Lighting at the Colorado Capital felt heavy. We are still processing the devastating shooting that occurred in Australia last Sunday. [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/m9UEWboAVzBa_Z54Z0nbOSpjiizI5ZxFvEIv-HeA-4MmCbjvJ08RIDfx749qrXKRv93X-yUnC5B4JSYonyc6nT5kRUISuTi8TbasX1lp5gnGT9_cnMU4A85Z5iJQPhY4dHbsL55EKtvNkAxx7vWKQxStftWaG1si5wxsU_dMWN5jUepJL6DLIW-QqDJfRULUmfjGY-DmsvC_15HokZbFmFwA49yhoHRVXmw5IzHrmHoLY15tKjCWfyZp43RbKqkUeuIVjBW6tIvdpcmTLqUQnBsDjjhlJgi8vAGI72kzofFsnoOwtXQJcO36Bczw1-t7FccU3e1J1_ZybfEECagsZ7QItrtjnQrKLcQ_pY2I7eMMlSRX52jpu0tZf5gM8EdsCIEeJXo/Bcx58v1R-6tkhLIXfzPcLoPMSj3zpSj7/16) The Jewish community of Sydney gathered on Bondi Beach to ring in the first night of Hanukkah when two gunmen opened fire - killing 15 and injuring at least 29. The rise in antisemitism has contributed to violence in communities around the world - including in Boulder just a few months back - and it must be condemned. Violence is never the answer. My heart is with the victims, their loved ones, and Jewish communities everywhere who are once again being forced to grieve in moments that should be filled with light and joy. I’ve learned through my wife that the beauty of the Jewish people lies in resilience - and we learn that lesson through the story of Hanukkah. So, even as we hurt for the Jewish community of Sydney and for Jews around the world - we gathered and we celebrated. We practiced centuries-old traditions. We educated and included allies, neighbors, and friends. Because that’s just what Jews do - even in the darkest of times. Hanukkah means dedication, and I can think of no better value to hold dear in these times. The Festival of Lights is a time to rededicate ourselves to resilience, hope, faith, community, and to working together to illuminate the path forward. May the remaining days of the holiday bring warmth to your homes, strength to your spirit, and resolve to us all. [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/TafVisKldHlajqIKGlXLXSuSeD2rJK29Ybx-_5HrU44dip0CxJc074PdGAeHCFI0UxgU0LuLE7XS3QyRVjh6kGEGQYqgcJOAMlFSiaWohIzCH_6qXWH5Il0BqpYGgHpFsCDsVq1Zro-yBuhkCoVFgpkMC2xNN6CAfziskEiWJ75K3TZXiYAAJiE2efOkqdt-y9bAwokaDAeoSimEnX8Zg4vgH9uDkfdJKWd6nulBPNF_8t2lQMRqsNhp8zOyZvVIfrS3KikxPFwtUtAmeFX-hpp03MwZQriaPUBYAYEe4AcUMs4p9zhFATFX9iQKeHCPhBb36io_fh23RYMp0wamqIfr_BDI82KaRS2pzKV2wQ8QGmJE5uJn29I3dyoWFWztDUr5Uvw/X0qQxabwCqIsqbi8qAagxQubC84oHDIf/16) Chag Semeach, and may the lights of Hanukkah shine brightly for you and your loved ones. Brianna
* * *

[](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/jBLIHSx98f8Yyx3VspPTAmudGBUQDCAdqQf95m0YkytwnxcmI3K3LUHj0prqJwJ3kNXMXSEvikm9X-KT85oqsJfq4pyHwmfM_e20jgEqw-gZjDARW6A2xP3IMQYchBj0pPmiHB0aaadj5jExOGRxzoz7FNYmxNp_WqOQ8sLlqGrU-NZYPu94xneBfwwzpCVJUU_wOtFBGD2rU3qfu0HE-yiXiJOsCenGxXGMQJmXjqUaObdUi_tWXJGPSPuO9ehtaunwm6um1RxVjaYfrRgyKqQc8Q6iEG4ltZSJ3kyV1Y1-rnbg1C8mQRlMj2-ylQIko1nPziIWTBZfaGa5GwxpAiBBgvyN87L9NheOoniGlcikpAwO3WqCSO5ux73cOxIY00NXsyYAykBluoDWdM8OxIDg4DG9iwXJ2UAJrCH-uklJ9xEvoA/XCMopmyrSYZakx9oXarD0HZtBFrplN5Z/16) clean_body: As we gather with loved ones and prepare for tomorrow’s celebrations, I want to wish you a Merry Christmas! | | | Hi Peter, Thursday’s Hanukkiah Lighting at the Colorado Capital felt heavy. We are still processing the devastating shooting that occurred in Australia last Sunday. The Jewish community of Sydney gathered on Bondi Beach to ring in the first night of Hanukkah when two gunmen opened fire - killing 15 and injuring at least 29. The rise in antisemitism has contributed to violence in communities around the world - including in Boulder just a few months back - and it must be condemned. Violence is never the answer. My heart is with the victims, their loved ones, and Jewish communities everywhere who are once again being forced to grieve in moments that should be filled with light and joy. I’ve learned through my wife that the beauty of the Jewish people lies in resilience - and we learn that lesson through the story of Hanukkah. So, even as we hurt for the Jewish community of Sydney and for Jews around the world - we gathered and we celebrated. We practiced centuries-old traditions. We educated and included allies, neighbors, and friends. Because that’s just what Jews do - even in the darkest of times. Hanukkah means dedication, and I can think of no better value to hold dear in these times. The Festival of Lights is a time to rededicate ourselves to resilience, hope, faith, community, and to working together to illuminate the path forward. May the remaining days of the holiday bring warmth to your homes, strength to your spirit, and resolve to us all. Chag Semeach, and may the lights of Hanukkah shine brightly for you and your loved ones. Brianna Follow Brianna Online: ** Paid for by Brianna for Treasurer. Registered Agent Brianna Titone.

Quick confession:

I’m a huge sneakerhead.

I wear a suit and tie to work in Congress, but rocking my Airforce 1s around Capitol Hill and back home has become my calling card.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121689460/590256036/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjkzMTRmNmYtZWZkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=uVYx8J8rx9QfZP3Zf5VuFS2o1iT0VVz87GVrepgHThs=)
Custom. Members only. 😏 2025 put some miles on these sneakers, and I’m not done yet.

**I’m now the most vulnerable House Democrat in California. In fact, CA-45 is ranked as one of the most competitive races in the entire country.**

Tough district or not, it hasn’t stopped me from speaking out against this administration’s lawless abuse of power.

Has it made me a target? _Absolutely._

Are more GOP challengers lining up? _You bet._

But I didn’t run for Congress to play it safe -- I ran to hold people in power accountable. And yes, I’ll do it in sneakers.

**[So here’s the ask: will you help me keep these sneakers on the House floor? clean_body: | = Quick confession: I’m a huge sneakerhead. I wear a suit and tie to work in Congress, but rocking my Airforce 1s around Capitol Hill and back home has become my calling card. = Custom. Members only. 😏 2025 put some miles on these sneakers, and I’m not done yet. I’m now the most vulnerable House Democrat in California. In fact, CA-45 is ranked as one of the most competitive races in the entire country. Tough district or not, it hasn’t stopped me from speaking out against this administration’s lawless abuse of power. Has it made me a target? Absolutely. Are more GOP challengers lining up? You bet. But I didn’t run for Congress to play it safe -- I ran to hold people in power accountable. body:
The Department of Defense is upgrading its preliminary review of Mark Kelly to an official investigation.

Folks, Mark Kelly here.

They are escalating. name: Voter Action Project
subject: Check out this map of important elections next year:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
These types of elections always fly under the media's radar, Team, but in 2026, 26 Secretary of State races will shape the future of our elections, redistricting in 2030, and the outcome of the 2028 presidential race.
Opponents of voting rights already know this, which is why they’re already pouring money into these elections – and we have to fight back now, while it still makes a difference. Check out this map:
Map of Key Secretary of State Races in 2026 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=122025_2&amount=25]
The work to increase voter participation and turnout for these races has to start now, not next summer. We have to build the infrastructure to defend pro-voter initiatives in Arizona, Michigan, Wisconsin, and Nevada, at the same time as we work to flip the script in key battleground states like Georgia, Ohio, and Iowa.
Team: None of it happens without your support. Fuel our work for next year's elections with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=122025_2&amount=25]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=122025_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=122025_2&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=122025_2&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=122025_2&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=122025_2&amount=250] Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=122025_2&amount=35]
Opponents of voting rights have been working on these objectives for years. They've targeted state legislatures and key statewide offices to control the rules around voting and that's why they've been in a position to follow up on the "Big Lie" with bill after bill suppressing the vote. They've played the long game to pick and choose their voters and protect their own power.
Most people don't know that their Secretary of State is the state official who runs their elections, let alone who the current office-holder in their state is. But they are the last line of defense against voter suppression and diminished ballot access , which is why we need to invest in voter information, registration, and participation in these critical races.
2025 has been a tough year but we're not backing down. Our right to vote is too important and too much is at stake for our democracy, our fellow Americans, and the direction of our country. Driving pro-voting rights voters to the polls in 2026 is the best possible response to all that we've been forced to live through the last decade.
Your gift -- $5, $10, $25, or more -- helps provide the resources we need to keep voters apprised of what is at stake and get them out to vote for pro-democracy policies. Please, chip in what you can today! >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=122025_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122025_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122025_2&amount=20]

Portland, OR 97280
United States
