clean_body: Trump's warning is clear: The push for a Digital Dollar isn’t about innovation. It’s about control — over your money, your privacy, your future. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Trump's warning is clear: The push for a Digital Dollar isn’t about innovation. It’s about control — over your money, your privacy, your future. This new “programmable” currency could track every transaction, restrict where and how you spend, and freeze your funds with a keystroke. And once it’s launched, there’s no going back. But there’s one shielded move left — real, physical assets like gold and silver held outside the digital system. Shielded. Untouchable. Yours. This might be your last chance to opt out. clean_body: Your endorsement matters more than any politicians – here's why. | | | | | | | | Peter, Everyone in politics loves to brag about endorsements. Senators. Congressmen. Influencers. Party leaders. And yes – Bryce Reeves could go out and get every big Republican endorsement in the country if he wanted to. But Bryce asked us to send this email because the endorsement he wants first is YOURS. Not Washington's. Not Richmond's. YOURS. Because this campaign isn't going to be won by insiders – it 's going to be won by the people who believe in Bryce's mission, Bryce's integrity, and Bryce's fight for Virginia. That's why we're launching the Official Endorse Bryce Petition, and Bryce wants YOUR name on it before anyone else's. | | | And to thank early supporters who step up in these opening days, we're doing something special: Add your name and donate $20 or more today, and we 'll send you 2 FREEReeves for U.S. Senate buttons. So you can show your support proudly – at work, at events, or anywhere the radical Left needs a reminder that Virginia is ready for a change. | | | | | | Bryce fights for Virginia every single day – with honesty, courage, and a backbone. Now he's asking for something simple: Your name. Your support. Your belief in this mission. Thank you, Team Reeves | | | | | | | | | | | | P.O. clean_body: Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement. In fact, we've made a unanimous decision. If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! Sincerely, Mike Johnson56th Speaker of the House On behalf of House GOP HQ (Platinum Committee)| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Michael Bennet
subject: Cleaner air and lower energy bills for CO families
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
| | |
---
Look, team –
Working families in Colorado deserve both cleaner air, _and_ more affordable and reliable energy service.
We've also got to provide our businesses and start-ups with a predictable, market-based path to cut greenhouse gas emissions.
And above all, we owe it to our kids to tackle climate change head-on, reach net-zero emissions ASAP and protect Colorado's unparalleled natural beauty.
**That's where my _Clean Air, Affordable Energy_ cap-and-invest program comes in – _[but if you're short on time, then before I explain, will you please rush $5 or more toward my ambitious $100,000 year-end goal so we can put it into action when we win the governor's race?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121690196/590258619/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk1016-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4N2UyMjA1YS1mOGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yxxNnIRL7WoqFtS8omNW3PoAkWBj88aYw2Jm7Eq_7Ho=)_**
Simply put: **There's no building opportunity for the next generation of Coloradans if we don't take steps now to leave behind a livable state.**
If we're going to meet the growing energy demand while sticking to our net-zero target by 2050, then we need a long-term strategy. So as governor, I'll enthusiastically throw my support behind:
* **Capping emissions** across major economic sectors,
* Giving businesses **reliable, efficient and flexible paths to compliance,**
* Incentivizing businesses to pursue **cleaner operations,**
* Allowing Colorado’s new program to **link to existing carbon markets** , and
* **Investing the proceeds** to support climate resilience, to develop cutting-edge, climate-friendly tech, to reduce harmful air pollution in disadvantaged regions and to expand sustainable purchase programs

– to keep Colorado a national leader in the fight to end climate change, while securing energy independence and our competitive edge in the marketplace.
I'm up for any ideas that lower costs, create jobs and bolster our environment. I'm ready and willing to convene residents, businesses, environmental leaders power providers, municipalities, Tribal Nations and labor from every corner of Colorado to flesh out this plan.
**But if we're going to lead the nation into a cleaner, greener future, then we have to demonstrate the grassroots strength of this campaign as we enter the election cycle – and it starts by closing the gap to my $100,000 goal by the final public reporting deadline on Dec. 31 at midnight.**

clean_body: | | | Look, team – Working families in Colorado deserve both cleaner air, and more affordable and reliable energy service. We've also got to provide our businesses and start-ups with a predictable, market-based path to cut greenhouse gas emissions. And above all, we owe it to our kids to tackle climate change head-on, reach net-zero emissions ASAP and protect Colorado's unparalleled natural beauty. That's where my Clean Air, Affordable Energy cap-and-invest program comes in – = Simply put: There's no building opportunity for the next generation of Coloradans if we don't take steps now to leave behind a livable state. If we're going to meet the growing energy demand while sticking to our net-zero target by 2050, then we need a long-term strategy. So as governor, I'll enthusiastically throw my support behind: Capping emissions across major economic sectors, Giving businesses reliable, efficient and flexible paths to compliance, Incentivizing businesses to pursue cleaner operations, Allowing Colorado’s new program to link to existing carbon markets , and Investing the proceeds to support climate resilience, to develop cutting-edge, climate-friendly tech, to reduce harmful air pollution in disadvantaged regions and to expand sustainable purchase programs – to keep Colorado a national leader in the fight to end climate change, while securing energy independence and our competitive edge in the marketplace. I'm up for any ideas that lower costs, create jobs and bolster our environment. I'm ready and willing to convene residents, businesses, environmental leaders power providers, municipalities, Tribal Nations and labor from every corner of Colorado to flesh out this plan. But if we're going to lead the nation into a cleaner, greener future, then we have to demonstrate the grassroots strength of this campaign as we enter the election cycle – and it starts by closing the gap to my $100,000 goal by the final public reporting deadline on Dec. 31 at midnight. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = Let's do this – together. Our kids and grandkids deserve nothing less. Michael | Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States Paid for by Bennet for Governor. Peter,

We’re 11 days from our final FEC deadline of the year, and we need a surge of support today to stay on track. Will you **[chip in $3](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121690259/590258801/148511826?refcode=em-12.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTk4NDlhNy1mN2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JsNLLIk09FY263oj4yJy7_Xx6Bx673AtlEKT1MpB_3Q=)** , then let me explain?

As we look toward the midterm election year, I’m reminded of what drove me to run for Congress in the first place. It was never my plan to run for office -- I’m not a career politician.

I served my community in other ways: Heading NASA under Obama, leading a local aerospace company where I created 700+ jobs in the community, and co-founding an organization dedicated to ending the wildfire crisis.

**I decided to step up when it was clear that our Republican representative was in Washington to act as a rubber stamp for the MAGA agenda -- not fight for the hardworking families struggling to make ends meet in CA-27.**

We faced strong political headwinds and over $15 MILLION in outside spending. Despite the odds and with your grassroots support, I was one of the only Democrats to flip a GOP seat in 2024.

Now, almost a year into my first term in Congress, the challenges we’re facing as a country and democracy are steep. I’m working hard to deliver results, but with the GOP majority bowing to Trump’s every demand, progress is slow.

It’s clear that Democrats must flip the majority to hold this administration accountable -- and it can’t be done without this competitive seat. As a first-time Congressman who flipped a GOP seat, I’m in a particularly vulnerable spot.

**[I need you with me: Will you show your support with a $3 donation ahead of our public filing deadline? We must start the new year with strength.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121690261/590258803/148511826?refcode=em-12.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTk4NDlhNy1mN2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JsNLLIk09FY263oj4yJy7_Xx6Bx673AtlEKT1MpB_3Q=)**

I’m personally selecting the first 500 Founding Members, and I want YOU! These 500 spots will NEVER be offered again. But there’s a problem only you can fix. The Radical Left is spending hundreds of millions to tear down everything we built. They want to erase our victories. They want to rewrite history so our movement never happened. I won’t let that happen. That’s why I’m creating the MAGA Hall of Fame, a commemoration of the patriots who saved America twice. I need 500 Founding Members ASAP. Once we hit 500, the list is sealed forever. No exceptions. Do this for our country. Do it tonight. Thank you, Donald J. Hello, Peter. It’s Kamala Harris reaching out to supporters of Sen. Jon Ossoff. One of the most important campaigns in 2026 is happening in Georgia to reelect Jon, and he is currently facing the last fundraising deadline before his election year. I called Jon last month to tell him that I will support his race in any way he needs. Today, I am making good on that promise by asking you to [**make a contribution to his reelection campaign before his end-of-year deadline.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251220-1-f&t=2&akid=1314%2E5691347%2E21pbEv) Real talk: I know the power is in the South. When I look at leaders like Jon, I know I am looking at the future of America. The path to winning back the Senate runs right through Georgia, and national Republicans will not stop in their pursuit to maintain their current majority. Theirs is a narrow, self-serving vision of America – where they punish truth-tellers like Jon, favor loyalists, profit from their positions, and leave everyone else to fend for themselves. We need Jon as a strong voice against corruption in the Senate. Peter, it is going to take all of us working together to fight for our country and help him win this race. So I am asking: [**Will you join Jon and me in this fight by making a $5 donation — or whatever you can afford — to Jon’s reelection campaign before his last FEC deadline of 2025? It would mean a lot to me.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251220-1-f&t=3&akid=1314%2E5691347%2E21pbEv) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | **[CONTRIBUTE $5](https://act.electjon.com/go/1686?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.zuSSTi&refcode=em251220-1-f&t=22&akid=1314%2E5691347%2E21pbEv)**

name: Shawnna Bolick
subject: December in Review: Policy Progress & Community Impact
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTE BOLICK
body:
From election integrity to housing and public safety, here’s what I’ve been working on.

| | | | | [|
---](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=c487d98ea9&e=a7ab36849c)
| | | | | |
# 📰 **December Legislative & Community Update **📰
As we wrap up 2025, I’m grateful for the opportunities to advance meaningful policy, strengthen community partnerships, and continue serving Arizona families.

December has been full of engagement — from national policy discussions to local business visits, public safety conversations, and preparing for the 2026 legislative session. Below are some of this December’s highlights.
🗳️ **Election Integrity & National Policy Engagement**I traveled to Texas to meet with the [Honest Elections Project](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=6c44325fba&e=a7ab36849c), continuing our work together since 2023 to ban foreign donations in Arizona elections. While in Fort Worth, I also attended two ALEC task force meetings focused on judiciary, elections, and legislative procedures.
🎮 **Supporting Local Small Businesses** I visited [Gamers Guild](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=75b937c83f&e=a7ab36849c) and [Forks & Boards](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=88ef697cea&e=a7ab36849c) on Bell Road to present a certificate recognizing their positive impact on the North Phoenix community. Fun fact: Gamers Guild is the largest tabletop gaming space in North America — offering a homegrown, inclusive environment with high‐quality food and beverages.
💼 **Preparing for 2026: Tax Conformity** One of the biggest issues next legislative session will be tax conformity following the federal government’s [HR1](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=5cb2b6de1c&e=a7ab36849c), signed by Trump in July 2025. The Senate and House currently have different views on which portions Arizona should adopt. Negotiations will be lively, but I’m hopeful we’ll reach a responsible solution.
🚓 **Public Safety & Criminal Justice **You may have seen recent news about the rise in law enforcement’s use of license plate readers (LPRs). I met with Goodyear’s Police Chief on these LPRs to learn how they are using these public safety tools responsibly. Because this technology isn’t defined in Arizona statute, expect the Senate Public Safety Committee to take this up in 2026. Several senators met with a representative from the county attorney’s office and several law enforcement agencies across the state to discuss increasing penalties on johns.
🔔 **Honoring Arizona’s History** I joined the ribbon cutting for the Arizona Migration Monument at Wesley Bolin Plaza. The historic bell featured in the monument once summoned a ferry across the Colorado River beginning in 1873 — symbolizing the faith and resilience of pioneers who helped shape our state. This bell was once hung at the Lonely Dell Ranch now hangs near the state capitol. This unique bell helped members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints along with countless others crossing the Colorado River at the confluence of the Paria River. This bell symbolizes the commitment and faith of those who sought new lives and liberty- in Arizona and across our country. For those pioneers who migrated South, the journey around the Grand Canyon was particularly long and arduous.
🚀 **Arizona Space Commission Holiday Gathering** I stopped by the Arizona Space Commission’s holiday celebration. Relaunched in 2024, the commission continues Arizona’s long‐standing commitment to aerospace innovation. This commission was originally established in the early 1990s.
👨👩👧 **Child Safety & Kayden’s Law**I met with the chief justice of the supreme court to discuss strengthening child protection in custody cases involving abuse or domestic violence. Prioritizing child safety remains a top focus of the Republican caucus heading into 2026.
🐾 **Animal Welfare & Workforce Development**Last session, I sponsored a bill to create an alternative pathway for certified veterinary technicians (CVT). I ended up using this bill as a strike everything to get an animal cruelty bill across the finish line. During the interim, we revived stakeholder meetings for the CVT bill. I recently met with a local dachshund rescue founder and the owner of [Vetter Care](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=e6ce3459b2&e=a7ab36849c). Together, the insights of multiple stakeholders will help refine the legislation for next session. 📜 **Legislative Update: SB 1032** I pre‐filed my second piece of legislation for 2026. [Senate Bill 1032](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=09592bef71&e=a7ab36849c), an appropriations bill to fund the independent corrections oversight office created last session. The Arizona Department of Corrections spends nearly $2 billion of our taxpayer dollars. Shouldn't we want more accountability of this agency?The governor removed this funding during the 2025 budget process, but accountability in our corrections system is too important to leave unfunded.You can read my joint press release with Rep. Blackman [HERE ](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=e0a526354a&e=a7ab36849c).

📰**Bolick in the News**
* [ KJZZ](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=0575355356&e=a7ab36849c)
* [State Affairs](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=16bf07f3c4&e=a7ab36849c)
* [Arizona Capitol Times](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=68a4e33efa&e=a7ab36849c)

🏘️ **Addressing Homelessness** I met with several Maricopa County nonprofits working to expand emergency shelter capacity. Their dedication to serving individuals experiencing homelessness is inspiring, and I’m committed to supporting solutions that prioritize safety, dignity, and long‐term stability.
🔄 **Touring New Freedom: Second Chances in Action** I toured New Freedom, a reentry facility in my former legislative district. I saw firsthand the powerful work happening there — from educational pathways to workforce development, résumé writing, interview preparation, and job placement for individuals recently released from prison.
🏡 **Regulatory Affairs & Housing Supply**As part of the Senate Majority’s 2026 legislative plan, I participated in a video outlining our priorities. Once it is released, I will share it with you.As chair of the Regulatory Affairs and Government Efficiency Committee, I emphasized the need to:
* Expand affordable housing
* Speed up permitting
* Reduce unnecessary regulation

Cutting red tape is essential to increasing housing supply and meeting Arizona’s growing demand.
---

---

---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | **👉 Please**[**c**](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=6801404b71&e=a7ab36849c)[**lick here**](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=4fb8656dc0&e=a7ab36849c)**** to donate to my campaign to keep our momentum going.
---

---
| | | | | |
### 📅 **Upcoming Events**
* **Campaign Fundraiser in downtown Phoenix** 📍 January 6th, 4–6 PM Please RSVP: [info@bolickforarizona.com](mailto:info@bolickforarizona.com?subject=RSVP%20for%20January%206th%20fundraiser&body=I%20would%20like%20to%20RSVP%20for%20her%20January%202026%20fundraiser.%20) to receive the address for the location.

As we enter the final days of 2025, I want to thank you for staying engaged and informed. As you know, I have officially filed paperwork to run for a **third term**. With your continued support, we will hold this Senate seat in November 2026 and keep fighting for Arizona families, small businesses, and educational freedom.Thank you for staying engaged and informed. Your interest and partnership helps ensure we continue building a stronger, safer, and more prosperous Arizona.
Best,
Shawnna

Negotiations will be lively, but I’m hopeful we’ll reach a responsible solution. 🚓 Public Safety & Criminal Justice You may have seen recent news about the rise in law enforcement’s use of license plate readers (LPRs). I met with Goodyear’s Police Chief on these LPRs to learn how they are using these public safety tools responsibly. Because this technology isn’t defined in Arizona statute, expect the Senate Public Safety Committee to take this up in 2026. Several senators met with a representative from the county attorney’s office and several law enforcement agencies across the state to discuss increasing penalties on johns. 🔔 Honoring Arizona’s History I joined the ribbon cutting for the Arizona Migration Monument at Wesley Bolin Plaza. The historic bell featured in the monument once summoned a ferry across the Colorado River beginning in 1873 — symbolizing the faith and resilience of pioneers who helped shape our state. This bell was once hung at the Lonely Dell Ranch now hangs near the state capitol. This unique bell helped members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints along with countless others crossing the Colorado River at the confluence of the Paria River. This bell symbolizes the commitment and faith of those who sought new lives and liberty- in Arizona and across our country. For those pioneers who migrated South, the journey around the Grand Canyon was particularly long and arduous. 🚀 Arizona Space Commission Holiday Gathering I stopped by the Arizona Space Commission’s holiday celebration. Relaunched in 2024, the commission continues Arizona’s long‐standing commitment to aerospace innovation. This commission was originally established in the early 1990s. 👨👩👧 Child Safety & Kayden’s LawI met with the chief justice of the supreme court to discuss strengthening child protection in custody cases involving abuse or domestic violence. Prioritizing child safety remains a top focus of the Republican caucus heading into 2026. 🐾 Animal Welfare & Workforce DevelopmentLast session, I sponsored a bill to create an alternative pathway for certified veterinary technicians (CVT). I ended up using this bill as a strike everything to get an animal cruelty bill across the finish line. During the interim, we revived stakeholder meetings for the CVT bill. I recently met with a local dachshund rescue founder and the owner of . Together, the insights of multiple stakeholders will help refine the legislation for next session. 📜 Legislative Update: SB 1032 I pre‐filed my second piece of legislation for 2026. , an appropriations bill to fund the independent corrections oversight office created last session. The Arizona Department of Corrections spends nearly $2 billion of our taxpayer dollars. Shouldn't we want more accountability of this agency?The governor removed this funding during the 2025 budget process, but accountability in our corrections system is too important to leave unfunded.You can read my joint press release with Rep. Blackman . 📰Bolick in the News 🏘️ Addressing Homelessness I met with several Maricopa County nonprofits working to expand emergency shelter capacity. Their dedication to serving individuals experiencing homelessness is inspiring, and I’m committed to supporting solutions that prioritize safety, dignity, and long‐term stability. 🔄 Touring New Freedom: Second Chances in Action I toured New Freedom, a reentry facility in my former legislative district. I saw firsthand the powerful work happening there — from educational pathways to workforce development, résumé writing, interview preparation, and job placement for individuals recently released from prison. 🏡 Regulatory Affairs & Housing SupplyAs part of the Senate Majority’s 2026 legislative plan, I participated in a video outlining our priorities. Once it is released, I will share it with you.As chair of the Regulatory Affairs and Government Efficiency Committee, I emphasized the need to: Expand affordable housing Speed up permitting Reduce unnecessary regulation Cutting red tape is essential to increasing housing supply and meeting Arizona’s growing demand. | | 📌 Campaign Update👉 Please** to sign my online nomination petition to help me qualify for the August Republican primary ballot. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | 👉 Please to donate to my campaign to keep our momentum going. | | | | | | | 📅 Upcoming Events Campaign Fundraiser in downtown Phoenix 📍 January 6th, 4–6 PM Please RSVP: mailto:info@bolickforarizona.com?subject=RSVP%20for%20January%206th%20fundraiser&body=I%20would%20like%20to%20RSVP%20for%20her%20January%202026%20fundraiser.%20 to receive the address for the location. As we enter the final days of 2025, I want to thank you for staying engaged and informed. As you know, I have officially filed paperwork to run for a third term. With your continued support, we will hold this Senate seat in November 2026 and keep fighting for Arizona families, small businesses, and educational freedom.Thank you for staying engaged and informed. Your interest and partnership helps ensure we continue building a stronger, safer, and more prosperous Arizona. Best, Shawnna | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Vote Bolick, All rights reserved. If you want to mail a donation, our mailing address is: Shawnna Bolick c/o Vote Bolick 610 E. Bell Road 2-142 Phoenix, AZ 85022 | | | | | | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY VOTE BOLICK. AUTHORIZED BY SHAWNNA BOLICK. | | | | | | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Vote Bolick c/o Shawnna Bolick 610 E. Bell Rd. #2-142 Phoenix, AZ 85022 USA

| | Team,

Health care costs are rising. And once again, _**it's hardworking families who are paying the price for the chaos in Washington. The urgency of this situation cannot be overstated.**_

What we need right now is commonsense problem solving, not partisan games.

Josh has always been about results. He’s a bipartisan leader who brings people together, sits down at the table, and actually gets things done — _whether it’s lowering taxes, restoring SALT, or delivering millions for Jersey’s infrastructure, first responders, and public safety._

Now more than ever, we need to fight to keep commonsense problem solvers like Josh in Congress. Extremists are hoping gridlock and dysfunction will open the door to flipping this seat — we can’t let that happen.

name: Team Cooper
subject: GOP is all about that Musk Money
body:
Help fight back >>>
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)
_Guess who’s back on team GOP, Peter..._ **That’s right. Elon Musk.** So much for his idea of launching his own party — we just learned that Musk is back in the Trump Administration’s inner circle and spending _untold sums_ on trying to buy seats to secure the GOP’s hold on power. Look, Musk is the richest man in the world. He can write the GOP a blank check if he wants to. In fact, Musk was the BIGGEST donor to either party in 2024, giving _hundreds of millions_ to Trump’s campaign and other Republican candidates. We have to be prepared for him to pour even more cash into the upcoming midterm elections. Since our race is the key to the U.S. Senate majority, we can only imagine what might be coming our way. We need your grassroots support NOW to fight back. **[Pitch in anything you can afford to help us take on GOP megadonors like Elon Musk>>>](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3208?t=7&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3209?t=8&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)

---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3214?t=13&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)
---
Thanks,
Team Cooper
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)**

Raleigh, NC 27602

Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=14&akid=213%2E124072%2EECOl20)

| | | Who would've thought that something SO easy, like putting [**this strong spice**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26281552/VaHR0cHM6Ly9xdWlldHVtcGx1cy5jb20vdmlkZW8ucGhw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjA/LbmNsYmdCU2FNbw/qP2FmZmlsaWF0ZT1ib2xkYXBleDEmdGlkPXBhcmxlcmRlZDE0RGVjMjVB/gaUc3JA/sdmdb7cea4d) under your tongue for a couple of minutes... Could make that excruciating ear ringing stop almost overnight... While also improving your brain capacity and memory! All without using toxic pills, dangerous surgeries or uncomfortable flushings. Ear doctors are totally clueless about how this [**dirt cheap method**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26281552/VaHR0cHM6Ly9xdWlldHVtcGx1cy5jb20vdmlkZW8ucGhw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjA/LbmNsYmdCU2FNbw/qP2FmZmlsaWF0ZT1ib2xkYXBleDEmdGlkPXBhcmxlcmRlZDE0RGVjMjVB/gaUc3JA/sdmdb7cea4d) works so perfectly. But the fact that over 45,000 patients got rid of the ear buzzing in a matter of minutes... Makes it too good not to try at least once! This page explains everything in simple steps: [**Strong Spice Under Your Tongue Instantly Stops the Ear Ringing**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26281552/VaHR0cHM6Ly9xdWlldHVtcGx1cy5jb20vdmlkZW8ucGhw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjA/LbmNsYmdCU2FNbw/qP2FmZmlsaWF0ZT1ib2xkYXBleDEmdGlkPXBhcmxlcmRlZDE0RGVjMjVB/gaUc3JA/sdmdb7cea4d) [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26281552/VaHR0cHM6Ly9xdWlldHVtcGx1cy5jb20vdmlkZW8ucGhw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMjA/LbmNsYmdCU2FNbw/qP2FmZmlsaWF0ZT1ib2xkYXBleDEmdGlkPXBhcmxlcmRlZDE0RGVjMjVB/gaUc3JA/sdmdb7cea4d)
